84	---
85	
86	## Core Design Principles
87	
88	1. **Agents communicate through files, not messages.** Planner writes `features.json` (projection only — see §File Contract), `architecture.md`, `handoff.json` seed. Implementer reads those, writes code + `progress.md` + appends to `handoff.json` + `.yoke/status-updates.jsonl`. Reviewer subagents write directly to `reviews/feature-N/<angle>.json`. No agent-to-agent message passing. No shared context.
89	
90	2. **Quality gates are the user's choice of mechanism; the harness just runs what you configure.** Users may wire quality gates via (a) a Claude Stop hook, (b) Yoke `post:` commands (D50), (c) both, or (d) neither at their own risk. The harness accepts phase completion when the configured acceptance conditions pass — the agent session exited cleanly, every `post:` command returned success, and every configured artifact validator passed. Yoke does not own a hook namespace. Yoke ships example templates users can copy; `yoke init` can scaffold them on opt-in. `.yoke/last-check.json` is an **optional convention**: if a user's Stop hook emits it, the dashboard displays its contents; the harness does not require its presence. [D28, D30, D50, D55]
91	
92	3. **Yoke orchestrates, Claude Code executes.** Yoke decides what to do next and whether previous work was valid. Claude Code does the work. Yoke never edits user code.
93	
94	4. **Claude-Code-native, unapologetically — but not jig-dependent.** stream-json, `-c`, Claude hooks, `Task` subagents are not behind an adapter. v1 assumes Claude Code. It does **not** assume `jig`: each phase declares the command it spawns (default `claude`); `jig` is a documented-recommended layer for agent-profile scoping, not a dependency. [D01, D55]
95	
96	5. **Token usage is observed, not managed — except for an opt-in pre-flight check.** Yoke tracks and reports usage. It does not throttle. A single opt-in usage gate per phase (v1.1) is the only exception. [D02]
97	
98	6. **SQLite is the single source of truth for workflow state.** The Pipeline Engine holds no durable in-memory state. Read, transition, write, re-read. Any in-memory object is a short-lived projection. [D03]
99	
100	7. **Phase names are labels, not enums.** The state machine knows transitions, not phase identities. Users define pipelines as graphs; Yoke does not hard-code "plan → implement → review." [D05]
101	
102	8. **Artifact integrity is structural, not prompted.** Rules about "agents must not edit X" are enforced by the harness diffing before/after, not by asking the agent nicely. [D10]
103	
104	---
105	
106	## Workflow Lifecycle
107	
108	### Phase: Plan
109	
110	A planner session, fresh context, read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep, LS).
111	
112	**Input:** user-provided spec (text/markdown/file) + `architecture.md` if present. If absent, planner drafts one. If present, planner reads it and may propose updates, written to `architecture-proposed.md` for user approval (never mutates `architecture.md` directly). [D54]
113	
114	**Output artifacts:**
115	- `features.json` — agent-produced, but a **projection only** (see §File Contract). Harness ingests into SQLite as canonical.
116	- `architecture-proposed.md` — if the planner wants to propose changes to an existing `architecture.md`.
117	- `init.sh` — optional bootstrap. Requires first-run user approval (threat model, §Threat Model).
118	
119	Planner defines `acceptance_criteria` and `review_criteria` per feature. Review criteria come from (a) planner-specific per-feature criteria, (b) configured review agent definitions (security, complexity, etc.).
120	
121	Harness validates: `features.json` parses, matches schema, ≥1 feature, each has description + acceptance criteria + review criteria, `depends_on` forms a DAG.
122	
123	### Phase: Implement (per feature, topologically ordered)
124	
125	A fresh implementer session per feature. Full tools (Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep + configured MCP).
126	
127	**Input prompt (via stdin, one-shot buffer):**
128	- Feature spec (pulled from SQLite, not worktree)
129	- Architecture.md (if present)
130	- `progress.md` (narrative)
131	- `handoff.json` entries relevant to this feature (structured cross-phase context, including any queued inject-context, retry history, reviewer notes from prior attempts)
132	- Recent git log (last 20 commits)
133	
134	**Output:**
135	- Code changes committed to git
136	- `progress.md` updated
137	- `.yoke/status-updates.jsonl` appended (narrow status channel — this is how the implementer reports status to SQLite)
138	- `handoff.json` entry for this attempt (intended_files, deferred_criteria, known_risks)
139	- `.yoke/last-check.json` — **optional**, written by a user-configured Stop hook if the user chose that quality-gate path
140	
141	**Permission mode:** Claude permission mode is whatever the user's command configuration requests (commonly `bypassPermissions` in agent workflows). Yoke does not set this; it passes through args/env to the spawned command.
142	
143	**Quality gates (user-configured, two paths):**
144	- **Claude Stop hook** (project-owned, lives in `.claude/hooks/` or equivalent) — runs typecheck/lint/test/build. Exit 2 blocks stop and feeds stderr back. Inner retry counter (if the user implements one) lives in `.yoke/hook-state.json`. [D24, D28, D31]
145	- **Yoke `post:` commands** (phase-level, see §Phase Pre/Post Commands) — harness runs after session exit. Exit 0 = pass; nonzero branches per D53. Lower-barrier than writing a hook.
146	
147	Users may use either path, both, or neither. Yoke `init` offers an optional quick-start that scaffolds example templates the user can keep, edit, or delete.
148	
149	**Retry:** when the session ends in failure, harness follows the outer-retry ladder: attempt 1 = `continue` (soft, `-c`-based); attempt 2 = `fresh_with_failure_summary` (handoff.json updated, new session); attempt 3 = `awaiting_user`. Configurable per phase. [D25]
150	
151	### Phase: Review (per feature)
152	
153	Orchestrator session spawns N subagent reviewers in sequence. Each subagent is scoped to read-only tools (Read, Bash, Glob, Grep) plus a narrow Write scoped to `reviews/feature-N/<angle>.json`. How that scoping is enforced depends on the user's command configuration — jig profiles are the recommended mechanism in the docs, but Claude's own tool-permission config or a PreToolUse deny hook are equally valid. The orchestrator never sees verdict content. [D14, D55]
154	
155	Harness aggregates after the orchestrator exits: deterministic `any(fail) → fail`. Aggregation lives in the Pipeline Engine, not in a prompt.
156	
157	Each reviewer verdict must cover every `acceptance_criteria` and `review_criteria` entry with explicit pass/fail+notes. Missing coverage → reject the review as `review_invalid`, re-run.
158	
159	On fail:
160	- Review-triggered re-implement defaults to a **fresh** session; review notes become handoff.json entries seeding it. Per-phase config override: `on_review_fail: { retry_mode: continue | fresh }`. [D56]
161	- Max review rounds configurable, default 2, then `awaiting_user`.
162	
163	### Completion
164	
165	When all features reach `complete` or `blocked`, harness runs the summary, notifies user (severity: `info`), optionally creates a PR.
166	
167	---
168	
169	## Phase Pre/Post Commands [D50, D53]
170	
171	A lightweight, configurable way to run arbitrary shell commands at phase boundaries with exit-code semantics. This is the primary mechanism for users who want enforcement without writing a Claude Stop hook, and the primary mechanism for phase-transition branching (replaces the hard-coded "iterative planner" affordance).
172	
173	### Semantics
174	
175	Each phase node in the pipeline may declare `pre:` and `post:` command arrays:
176	
177	- `pre:` runs in the worktree **before** Yoke spawns the agent for that phase. A failing `pre:` step blocks the phase from starting. Typical use: env validation, git cleanliness, port availability.
178	- `post:` runs in the worktree **after** the agent session exits (exit 0 AND no stream-json error frames). `post:` commands run sequentially. A command's exit code maps to an **action** declared in that command's spec.
179	
180	Both `pre:` and `post:` commands run with `spawn(cmd, args, {shell: false})`, CWD = worktree path, stdout/stderr captured to the session log and surfaced in the dashboard (same rendering path as `SystemNotice`), wall-clock timeout configurable per command (default 15 min, same ceiling as hooks).
181	
182	No polyglot runner: Yoke runs whatever shell command you give it and reads `$?`. Users bring their own static analyzers, test runners, schema validators, etc.
183	
184	### Action grammar
185	
186	Each command declares `actions: { <exit-code or wildcard> → <action> }`. Supported actions:
187	
188	| Action | Effect |
189	|---|---|
190	| `continue` | proceed to next command in the same array, or on the last command, fire `complete` event for the phase |
191	| `goto: <phase-name>` | absolute jump: next phase becomes `<phase-name>` |
192	| `goto-offset: +N` / `goto-offset: -N` | relative jump in declared graph order (allows "go back one step") |
193	| `retry: { mode, max }` | re-run the phase with the given retry mode; `mode` is `continue \| fresh_with_failure_summary \| fresh_with_diff`; exhaustion → `awaiting_user` |
194	| `stop-and-ask` | set workflow to `awaiting_user` with a message |
195	| `stop` | terminal: workflow abandoned |
196	| `fail: { reason }` | phase fails; normal outer retry ladder applies |
197	
198	Loop guard: each `goto*` action supports `max_revisits: N` (default 3) per destination. Exceeding it collapses to `awaiting_user` to prevent infinite loops.
199	
200	### Example (iterative planning via post-command, not hardcoded)
201	
202	```yaml
203	phases:
204	  plan:
205	    command: claude
206	    args: ["-p", "--output-format", "stream-json"]
207	    prompt_template: "prompts/plan.md"
208	    output_artifacts:
209	      - { path: "features.json", schema: "schemas/features.schema.json", required: true }
210	    post:
211	      - name: "check-needs-more-planning"
212	        run: ["jq", "-e", ".needs_more_planning == true", "features.json"]
213	        actions:
214	          0: { goto: "plan", max_revisits: 3 }   # 0 = the jq predicate matched; re-plan
215	          1: "continue"                          # 1 = predicate false; proceed
216	          "*": { fail: { reason: "planning-check errored" } }
217	
218	  implement:
219	    command: claude
220	    args: ["-p", "--output-format", "stream-json"]
221	    prompt_template: "prompts/implement.md"
222	    pre:
223	      - name: "worktree-clean"
224	        run: ["git", "diff", "--quiet"]
225	        actions: { 0: "continue", "*": "stop-and-ask" }
226	    post:
227	      - name: "static-analysis"
228	        run: ["./scripts/check.sh"]
229	        actions:
230	          0: "continue"
231	          "*": { retry: { mode: "continue", max: 1 } }
232	      - name: "surface-claude-hook-manifest"    # optional: user has a Stop hook writing .yoke/last-check.json
233	        run: ["test", "-f", ".yoke/last-check.json"]
234	        actions: { 0: "continue", "*": "continue" }   # non-fatal; display only
235	```
236	
237	The example is illustrative — none of the commands, artifacts, or action choices are hard-coded into the harness. Users with a different workflow wire different commands and actions.
238	
239	### Interaction with Claude Stop hooks
240	
241	Both paths can coexist. A user with a mature Claude Stop hook already enforcing tests/lint/build may skip `post:` entirely. A user who wants to stay out of hooks can drive all gating through `post:`. A user who wants layered defense can do both — the Stop hook gates the session, and a `post:` command asserts `.yoke/last-check.json` shape as a secondary check. Yoke treats both as equivalent inputs to phase completion.
242	
243	---
244	
245	## State Machine (new in draft3)
246	
247	### States
248	
249	| State | Meaning |
250	|---|---|
251	| `pending` | feature not yet started |
252	| `ready` | dependencies complete, eligible to start |
253	| `bootstrapping` | worktree bootstrap running |
254	| `bootstrap_failed` | terminal until user action; no auto-cleanup |
255	| `in_progress` | implementer session active |
256	| `awaiting_retry` | between failed attempt and next attempt (backoff window) |
257	| `review` | reviewer orchestrator running |
258	| `review_invalid` | reviewer output failed schema validation; re-run |
259	| `rate_limited` | Claude Code signaled rate limit; waiting with backoff |
260	| `awaiting_user` | all retries exhausted or policy requires human decision |
261	| `blocked` | manual/dependency block; user-reachable with "retry with notes" |
262	| `complete` | all reviews passed |
263	| `abandoned` | user cancelled or workflow aborted |
264	
265	### Transition table
266	
267	Implemented as a TypeScript `const` with a unit test asserting every `(state, event)` pair has a defined transition. Draft3 ships the full table as `src/server/state-machine/transitions.ts`; critical rows summarized here:
268	
269	```
270	(pending, deps_satisfied)             → ready
271	(ready, implement_phase_start)        → bootstrapping → in_progress
272	(in_progress, session_ok + manifest)  → review
273	(in_progress, session_fail + budget)  → awaiting_retry
274	(in_progress, rate_limit_detected)    → rate_limited
275	(awaiting_retry, backoff_done)        → in_progress
276	(rate_limited, probe_ok)              → in_progress
277	(review, all_verdicts_pass)           → complete
278	(review, any_verdict_fail + budget)   → in_progress (fresh)
279	(review, verdict_missing_or_invalid)  → review_invalid
280	(review_invalid, rerun)               → review
281	(*, retries_exhausted)                → awaiting_user
282	(awaiting_user, user_retry)           → in_progress
283	(awaiting_user, user_block)           → blocked
284	(blocked, user_unblock_with_notes)    → in_progress (fresh + handoff)
285	(*, user_cancel)                      → abandoned
286	```
287	
288	Every feature failure runs through the **failure classifier**: `transient | permanent | policy | unknown`. Transient uses exponential backoff in a retry window, not a monotonic counter. Default unknown → `awaiting_user`. [D07]
289	
290	### Dependency cascading
291	
292	Topological sort is v1. On `in_progress → awaiting_user` or `blocked`, the harness marks all transitive dependents with `blocked` + `blocked_reason: "dependency <feat-id> <status>"`. [D08]
293	
294	---
295	
296	## File Contract
297	
298	### features.json (in worktree — **read-only projection**)
299	
300	The canonical feature store is the SQLite `features` table. `features.json` in the worktree is a snapshot written by the harness before each phase and deleted after. The implementer may **read** it but not write it.
301	
302	Status updates from the implementer go through a narrow channel: the implementer appends lines to `.yoke/status-updates.jsonl`. Harness parses after session end and updates SQLite. The harness post-phase diff check rejects any non-whitespace change to `features.json` itself. [D10]
303	
304	```json
305	{
306	  "project": "project-name",
307	  "created": "2026-04-11T12:00:00Z",
308	  "features": [
309	    {
310	      "id": "feat-001",
311	      "category": "auth",
312	      "description": "User can log in with email and password",
313	      "priority": 1,
314	      "depends_on": [],
315	      "acceptance_criteria": [
316	        "Login form accepts email and password",
317	        "Invalid credentials show error message",
318	        "Successful login redirects to dashboard"
319	      ],
320	      "review_criteria": [
321	        "No credentials stored in plaintext",
322	        "Rate limiting on login attempts",
323	        "Input sanitization on all form fields"
324	      ],
325	      "status": "pending",
326	      "current_phase": "plan",
327	      "blocked_reason": null,
328	      "implemented_in_commit": null,
329	      "created_by_session_id": "sess-abc",
330	      "last_updated_by_session_id": "sess-abc"
331	    }
332	  ]
333	}
334	```
335	
336	### .yoke/status-updates.jsonl (implementer → harness)
337	
338	```jsonl
339	{"feature_id":"feat-001","status":"review","commit":"abc1234","ts":"..."}
340	```
341	
342	### handoff.json (structured cross-phase channel)
343	
344	Per-workflow, append-only. Solves the "progress.md is free-form and reviewers need context" leak. [D13]
345	
346	```json
347	{
348	  "feature_id": "feat-001",
349	  "entries": [
350	    {
351	      "phase": "implement",
352	      "attempt": 1,
353	      "session_id": "sess-abc",
354	      "ts": "...",
355	      "intended_files": ["src/auth/login.ts", "src/auth/login.test.ts"],
356	      "deferred_criteria": [],
357	      "known_risks": ["bcrypt cost factor hardcoded at 10"],
358	      "retry_history": [],
359	      "reviewer_notes_seen": [],
360	      "user_injected_context": null
361	    }
362	  ]
363	}
364	```
365	
366	User "inject context" entries queue here as `user_injected_context` and are consumed by the next session of that feature. [D43]
367	
368	### progress.md
369	
370	Free-form narrative. Not a contract. Exists alongside handoff.json.
371	
372	### reviews/feature-N/<angle>.json
373	
374	Each reviewer subagent writes its own file. Schema is fixed:
375	
376	```json
377	{
378	  "feature_id": "feat-001",
379	  "reviewer": "security",
380	  "reviewed_commit": "abc1234",
381	  "verdict": "fail",
382	  "acceptance_criteria_verdicts": [
383	    {"criterion": "Login form accepts email and password", "pass": true, "notes": ""}
384	  ],
385	  "review_criteria_verdicts": [
386	    {"criterion": "No credentials stored in plaintext", "pass": true, "notes": ""}
387	  ],
388	  "additional_issues": [
389	    {"severity": "high", "category": "security", "description": "...", "file": "...", "suggestion": "..."}
390	  ]
391	}
392	```
393	
394	Every planner-defined criterion must appear in its verdicts array. Harness rejects incomplete reviews as `review_invalid`.
395	
396	---
397	
398	## Protocol Layer — stream-json parsing + WebSocket
399	
400	### stream-json parsing [D15, D16]
401	
402	**Framing:** NDJSON (one JSON object per line, `\n`-delimited). ⚠ empirical verification required in Phase γ research (see runbook).
403	
404	**Parser:**
405	- Line-buffered reader: accumulate bytes in a `Buffer`, split on `\n`, parse complete lines, carry the trailing partial forward.
406	- Use `readline.createInterface({ input: child.stdout, crlfDelay: Infinity })` or a hand-rolled splitter. **Never** `JSON.parse` raw `data` chunks.
407	- Parse failure → log + skip + set `sessions.status_flags.parse_errors += 1`, do not crash pipeline.
408	- Line > 16 MB → truncate, mark session tainted with an `event`.
409	- stderr is a separate stream — never mixed into the JSON parser.
410	
411	**Token usage:** extracted from stream-json events, written to `sessions` columns:
412	- `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `raw_usage TEXT`
413	- Resumed sessions chain via `parent_session_id`; per-session = delta, per-feature = sum across chain.
414	- Exact event carrying usage → **to verify in Phase γ research**.
415	
416	### WebSocket envelope [D17]
417	
418	**Server → client:**
419	```ts
420	interface ServerFrame {
421	  v: 1;
422	  type: ServerFrameType;
423	  workflowId: string;
424	  sessionId?: string;
425	  seq: number;        // monotonic per session
426	  ts: string;
427	  payload: unknown;
428	}
429	```
430	Types: `hello`, `workflow.snapshot`, `workflow.update`, `feature.update`, `session.started`, `session.ended`, `stream.chunk`, `stream.tool_use`, `stream.tool_result`, `stream.text`, `stream.thinking`, `stream.usage`, `stream.system_notice`, `notice`, `error`, `pong`, `backfill.truncated`, `workflow.index.update`.
431	
432	**Client → server:**
433	```ts
434	interface ClientFrame {
435	  v: 1;
436	  type: 'subscribe' | 'unsubscribe' | 'control' | 'ack' | 'ping';
437	  id: string;    // client-generated commandId (idempotency)
438	  payload: unknown;
439	}
440	```
441	
442	On connect, server emits `hello { v: 1, serverVersion }`. Client refuses mismatched version instead of silent garbage rendering.
443	
444	### Backfill on reconnect [D19]
445	
446	Client sends `subscribe { workflowId, sinceSeq? }`. Server replies with `workflow.snapshot` + bounded backfill from the Session Log Store, then switches to live. If `sinceSeq` is older than retained buffer, server returns `backfill.truncated` with an HTTP URL for paged fetch. Client dedupes by `(sessionId, seq)`.
447	
448	### Subscription model [D20]
449	
450	Single multiplexed socket per client. Explicit `subscribe`/`unsubscribe` by workflowId. Server only sends subscribed workflows + a lightweight `workflow.index.update` for the sidebar list. Cap: 4 concurrent streaming subscriptions; extras degrade to polled snapshots.
451	
452	---
453	
454	## Client Render Model [D18]
455	
456	The client reduces wire frames into normalized events, distinct from the wire format:
457	
458	| Event | Behavior |
459	|---|---|
460	| `TextBlock` | Mutable; deltas accumulate until `content_block_stop`, then frozen. Rendered as a stable component with mutable text ref. |
461	| `ToolCall` | Tool name, input JSON, status `pending → running → ok/error`, linked to `tool_result` by `tool_use_id`. Collapsible. |
462	| `ThinkingBlock` | Collapsed by default, muted treatment. |
463	| `SystemNotice` | Session start/end, retries, hook failures, rate-limit detection, harness state transitions. Left-rule accent, severity colored. [D49 feeds into this] |
464	| `UsageUpdate` | Not rendered inline; feeds the HUD. |
465	
466	Virtualization: `@tanstack/react-virtual` with variable-size + measure-on-mount. Follow-tail within N pixels of bottom; detach on upscroll; "Jump to latest" pill. Text deltas buffered in 16ms rAF flush. Hard cap 10k events in-memory; older events paged from Session Log Store. Persistent scroll position across tab switches. [D41]
467	
468	---
469	
470	## Hooks Integration [D55]
471	
472	Yoke does **not** own a hook directory. Claude Code's hooks are the project's own — installed wherever Claude Code expects them (e.g., `.claude/hooks/`), managed however the user manages them (plain files, jig profiles, etc.). Yoke's job is to *interoperate* with whatever the user configures, not to install or replace it.
473	
474	### What Yoke provides
475	
476	1. **Example templates** in `docs/templates/hooks/`. Users copy these into their Claude hook directory if they want a quick-start. `yoke init` may offer to scaffold them on opt-in; it never overwrites existing hooks.
477	2. **A conventional data channel** — any Claude Stop hook that writes `.yoke/last-check.json` in the worktree will have that manifest surfaced in the dashboard and (if the user declares it) validated via `post:` commands. The manifest shape is documented; the channel is **optional** — Yoke does not require it.
478	3. **Acceptance rules** for a phase (see Principle #2): agent exit clean + all `post:` commands passed + all configured artifact validators passed. Nothing more, nothing less.
479	
480	### Optional: `.yoke/last-check.json` manifest
481	
482	Users who want a structured manifest from a Claude Stop hook into the dashboard can emit this shape:
483	
484	```json
485	{
486	  "hook_version": "1",
487	  "ran_at": "...",
488	  "gates": [
489	    { "name": "typecheck", "ok": true, "duration_ms": 1203 },
490	    { "name": "lint",      "ok": true, "duration_ms": 890 },
491	    { "name": "test",      "ok": true, "duration_ms": 5420, "test_count": 42, "pass_count": 42 },
492	    { "name": "build",     "ok": true, "duration_ms": 8100 }
493	  ]
494	}
495	```
496	
497	If present, dashboard renders it as part of the phase summary. If absent, nothing breaks — the user is either gating through `post:` commands instead or has chosen not to gate. No tamper check runs by default; a user wanting one configures a `post:` command that asserts checksums of their hook files.
498	
499	### Example-template quality gating (opt-in)

1196 /Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/docs/idea/plan-draft3.md
[rerun: b1]

500	### Example-template quality gating (opt-in)
501	
502	The bundled example template set is designed to work out of the box if the user opts in at `yoke init`:
503	
504	- **PreToolUse safety template** — denies writes outside the worktree path, denies reads of `~/.ssh`, `~/.aws`, `~/.config/gh`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.gnupg`, and curated Bash deny-list patterns. `safety_mode: strict | default | yolo` toggles aggressiveness.
505	- **Stop quality template** — runs project-type-detected typecheck/lint/test/build commands and emits `.yoke/last-check.json`.
506	
507	These are examples. Users may edit, delete, or ignore them entirely. Yoke does not re-install them on subsequent runs.
508	
509	### Hook failure modes (when a user *does* install hooks)
510	
511	Documented in `docs/research/hook-semantics.md` after Phase γ. Yoke's interaction surface with hooks is: it spawns the agent, the agent (or Claude runtime) invokes hooks, the agent session exits. Yoke interprets the exit as it would any other session outcome. Hook *internals* are the user's problem.
512	
513	### Hook exit code semantics (to verify in Phase γ research) [D29]
514	
515	Research task captures real Claude Code hook behavior: exit code meanings, stdin JSON schema, stdout JSON schema, timeout behavior. Documented in `docs/research/hook-semantics.md`. Yoke templates refer to that document rather than hard-coding guesses.
516	
517	---
518	
519	## Worktree Management [D40]
520	
521	### Lifecycle
522	
523	```
524	User starts workflow "add-auth"
525	  │
526	  ├─► Harness creates worktree at .worktrees/yoke-add-auth-<shortid>
527	  │   (branch: yoke/add-auth-<shortid>, never reused)
528	  │
529	  ├─► Runs bootstrap commands (own phase in state machine)
530	  │    on success → ready
531	  │    on failure → bootstrap_failed (no auto-cleanup; user inspects)
532	  │
533	  ├─► All agent sessions run in the worktree directory
534	  │
535	  ├─► Commits land on yoke/add-auth-<shortid>
536	  │
537	  ├─► On completion: harness can create PR
538	  │
539	  └─► Cleanup: kill tracked child pids → run .yoke/teardown.sh →
540	      git worktree remove --force → branch handling
541	```
542	
543	### Rules
544	
545	- **Branch naming:** always suffix with short workflow id; never reuse.
546	- **Bootstrap atomicity:** bootstrap_failed is a terminal state pending user action. No auto-cleanup.
547	- **Gitignored state reproduction:** bootstrap is responsible for reproducing `.env`, containers, etc. Document this clearly in the config guide.
548	- **Teardown:** `.yoke/teardown.sh` runs before `git worktree remove` — stops containers, closes sockets, etc.
549	- **Cleanup order:** kill children → teardown → remove worktree → handle branch.
550	- **Unpushed commits:** never auto-cleanup a worktree whose branch has unpushed commits unless PR creation succeeded.
551	
552	### Configuration
553	
554	```yaml
555	worktrees:
556	  base_dir: ".worktrees"
557	  branch_prefix: "yoke/"
558	  auto_cleanup: true
559	  cleanup_tool: "git"       # git | lazyworktree | custom
560	  bootstrap:
561	    commands:
562	      - "pnpm install"
563	  teardown:
564	    script: ".yoke/teardown.sh"
565	```
566	
567	---
568	
569	## Process Management
570	
571	### Spawning [D21, D22, D55]
572	
573	Spawn is command-agnostic. Each phase declares `command: string` and `args: string[]` in the pipeline config. Default is `claude`. Users who want profile scoping configure `command: "jig"` with `args: ["run", "<profile>", "--", ...]`.
574	
575	```ts
576	const child = spawn(phase.command, phase.args, {
577	  cwd: worktreePath,
578	  stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
579	  env: { ...process.env, ...phaseEnv, ...correlationEnv },
580	  detached: true,       // own process group for clean shutdown
581	});
582	
583	child.stdin.on('error', handleEpipe);
584	child.stdin.end(promptBuffer);  // one-shot, in-memory
585	
586	const lines = readline.createInterface({ input: child.stdout, crlfDelay: Infinity });
587	lines.on('line', parseStreamJsonLine);
588	
589	child.stderr.on('data', captureStderrWithCap);
590	child.on('error', handleSpawnError);  // ENOENT lands here, not stderr
591	child.on('exit', handleExit);
592	```
593	
594	Prompt size ceiling validated at assembly time (default 4 MB). Larger → phase fails with a clear error.
595	
596	### Shutdown
597	
598	- SIGTERM on the process group: `process.kill(-child.pid, 'SIGTERM')`.
599	- Wait 10 s.
600	- SIGKILL escalation: `process.kill(-child.pid, 'SIGKILL')`.
601	- `sessions` rows carry `{pid, pgid, started_at}` for post-crash reaping.
602	- `yoke doctor` command lists dangling processes.
603	
604	### Heartbeat [D23]
605	
606	Two signals, not one:
607	1. **Liveness** — PID alive AND (if inside a tool_use) the tool hasn't exceeded its per-tool wall-clock budget.
608	2. **Stream activity** — stdout-silence threshold applies **only outside** a tool_use.
609	
610	Heartbeat **never kills**. It emits a `SystemNotice` warning. User decides. Defaults: activity-timeout 90 s outside tool_use; per-tool budgets configurable.
611	
612	---
613	
614	## Crash Recovery [D26, D27]
615	
616	### Core rule
617	
618	`-c` is a soft optimization for intra-phase continuation. **Crash recovery does not depend on `-c`.** Recovery is always a fresh session; the harness reconstructs context from artifacts + `progress.md` + `handoff.json` + git log + Session Log Store.
619	
620	### On harness SIGTERM/SIGINT
621	
622	1. Every transition is already persisted (SQLite + fsync'd WAL); nothing to save.
623	2. SIGTERM each child process group.
624	3. Wait 10 s; SIGKILL stragglers.
625	4. Exit.
626	
627	### On harness restart
628	
629	1. Load workflow state from SQLite.
630	2. For each `in_progress` workflow:
631	   - Probe each session PID with `kill(pid, 0)`; mark stale sessions `cancelled`.
632	   - Inspect worktree: if `git status` is dirty, auto-stash as `yoke-crash-stash-<ts>` (configurable to `commit-wip`). Surface stash in UI.
633	   - For `review` state: delete any partial `reviews/feature-N/*` files so re-run starts clean.
634	   - Enter `awaiting_user` by default: user clicks "Resume" to fire a fresh session (or "Cancel").
635	   - Optionally auto-resume if `auto_resume: true` is set; default is `false` (laptop-primary target expects user acknowledgment after wake). [D60]
636	
637	### Dashboard surface
638	
639	Workflow cards show a `recoveryState` banner after a restart: `{ recoveredAt, priorStatus, resumeMethod, uncommittedChanges, lastKnownSessionId }`. Persists until user acknowledges.
640	
641	---
642	
643	## SQLite Schema [D38]
644	
645	WAL mode explicit. All state transitions wrapped in transactions. Dashboard reads use a separate read-only connection.
646	
647	```sql
648	PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;
649	PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL;
650	PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;
651	
652	CREATE TABLE schema_migrations (
653	  version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
654	  applied_at TEXT NOT NULL
655	);
656	
657	CREATE TABLE workflows (
658	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
659	  name TEXT NOT NULL,
660	  spec TEXT NOT NULL,
661	  pipeline TEXT NOT NULL,       -- JSON: phase graph
662	  config TEXT NOT NULL,         -- JSON: resolved config
663	  status TEXT NOT NULL,
664	  worktree_path TEXT,
665	  branch_name TEXT,
666	  recovery_state TEXT,          -- JSON when applicable
667	  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
668	  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
669	);
670	CREATE INDEX idx_workflows_status ON workflows(status);
671	
672	CREATE TABLE features (
673	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
674	  workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id),
675	  feature_data TEXT NOT NULL,   -- JSON: canonical feature object
676	  status TEXT NOT NULL,
677	  current_phase TEXT,           -- free-text label
678	  depends_on TEXT NOT NULL,     -- JSON array of feature ids
679	  retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
680	  retry_window_start TEXT,      -- for transient backoff
681	  created_by_session_id TEXT,
682	  last_updated_by_session_id TEXT,
683	  blocked_reason TEXT,
684	  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
685	);
686	CREATE INDEX idx_features_workflow ON features(workflow_id);
687	CREATE INDEX idx_features_status ON features(workflow_id, status);
688	
689	CREATE TABLE sessions (
690	  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
691	  workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id),
692	  feature_id TEXT REFERENCES features(id),
693	  parent_session_id TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id),  -- for -c chains
694	  phase TEXT NOT NULL,
695	  agent_profile TEXT NOT NULL,
696	  pid INTEGER,
697	  pgid INTEGER,
698	  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
699	  ended_at TEXT,
700	  exit_code INTEGER,
701	  input_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
702	  output_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
703	  cache_creation_input_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
704	  cache_read_input_tokens INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
705	  raw_usage TEXT,
706	  session_log_path TEXT,
707	  status TEXT NOT NULL,
708	  status_flags TEXT,            -- JSON: parse_errors, tainted, manifest_missing, etc.
709	  last_event_at TEXT,
710	  last_event_type TEXT
711	);
712	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_workflow ON sessions(workflow_id, feature_id);
713	
714	CREATE TABLE events (
715	  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
716	  ts TEXT NOT NULL,
717	  workflow_id TEXT,
718	  feature_id TEXT,
719	  session_id TEXT,
720	  phase TEXT,
721	  attempt INTEGER,
722	  event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
723	  level TEXT NOT NULL,          -- debug|info|warn|error
724	  message TEXT NOT NULL,
725	  extra TEXT                    -- JSON
726	);
727	CREATE INDEX idx_events_workflow ON events(workflow_id, ts);
728	
729	CREATE TABLE artifact_writes (
730	  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
731	  session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id),
732	  artifact_path TEXT NOT NULL,
733	  written_at TEXT NOT NULL,
734	  sha256 TEXT NOT NULL
735	);
736	CREATE INDEX idx_artifact_writes_session ON artifact_writes(session_id);
737	
738	CREATE TABLE pending_attention (
739	  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
740	  workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id),
741	  kind TEXT NOT NULL,           -- e.g. "blocked_feature", "crash_recovery_required"
742	  payload TEXT NOT NULL,        -- JSON
743	  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
744	  acknowledged_at TEXT
745	);
746	```
747	
748	Migration runner: forward-only, numbered SQL files in `migrations/`, run in a transaction on startup.

752	## Configuration
753	
754	```yaml
755	# .yoke.yml
756	version: "1"
757	
758	project:
759	  name: "my-saas-app"
760	
761	# Pipeline: phase graph (not just a list)
762	pipeline:
763	  nodes:
764	    plan:       { phase: plan }
765	    implement:  { phase: implement }
766	    review:     { phase: review }
767	  edges:
768	    - { from: plan,       on: complete, to: implement }
769	    - { from: implement,  on: complete, to: review }
770	    - { from: review,     on: pass,     to: complete }
771	    - { from: review,     on: fail,     to: implement }
772	
773	phases:
774	  plan:
775	    command: "claude"      # or: "jig" with args ["run","planner","--",...]
776	    args: ["-p", "--output-format", "stream-json"]
777	    prompt_template: "prompts/plan.md"
778	    output_artifacts:
779	      - { path: "features.json", schema: "schemas/features.schema.json", required: true }
780	    max_outer_retries: 2
781	    pre: []
782	    post:
783	      - name: "check-needs-more-planning"
784	        run: ["jq", "-e", ".needs_more_planning == true", "features.json"]
785	        actions:
786	          0: { goto: "plan", max_revisits: 3 }
787	          1: "continue"
788	          "*": { fail: { reason: "planning-check errored" } }
789	
790	  implement:
791	    command: "claude"
792	    args: ["-p", "--output-format", "stream-json"]
793	    prompt_template: "prompts/implement.md"
794	    output_artifacts:
795	      - { path: "progress.md", required: true }
796	    max_outer_retries: 3
797	    retry_ladder: ["continue", "fresh_with_failure_summary", "awaiting_user"]
798	    on_review_fail: { retry_mode: "fresh_with_failure_summary" }
799	    heartbeat:
800	      activity_timeout_s: 90
801	      per_tool_budgets:
802	        Bash: 900
803	        Edit: 30
804	    pre:
805	      - name: "worktree-clean"
806	        run: ["git", "diff", "--quiet"]
807	        actions: { 0: "continue", "*": "stop-and-ask" }
808	    post:
809	      - name: "static-analysis"  # or any command the user wants as a quality gate
810	        run: ["./scripts/check.sh"]
811	        actions:
812	          0: "continue"
813	          "*": { retry: { mode: "continue", max: 1 } }
814	
815	  review:
816	    command: "claude"
817	    args: ["-p", "--output-format", "stream-json"]
818	    prompt_template: "prompts/review.md"
819	    max_review_rounds: 2
820	    output_artifacts:
821	      - { path: "reviews/feature-{feature_id}/", required: true }
822	
823	worktrees:
824	  base_dir: ".worktrees"
825	  branch_prefix: "yoke/"
826	  auto_cleanup: true
827	  cleanup_tool: "git"
828	  bootstrap:
829	    commands:
830	      - "pnpm install"
831	
832	notifications:
833	  enabled: true
834	  severity_map:
835	    workflow_complete: info
836	    feature_complete: info
837	    workflow_failed: requires_attention
838	    feature_blocked: requires_attention
839	    rate_limited: requires_attention
840	    crash_recovered: requires_attention
841	  mechanisms:
842	    - type: browser_push
843	    - type: macos_native
844	
845	github:
846	  enabled: true
847	  auto_pr: true
848	  pr_target_branch: "main"
849	  auth_order: ["env:GITHUB_TOKEN", "gh:auth:token"]
850	  attach_artifacts_to_pr: true
851	  link_issues: true
852	
853	retention:
854	  sqlite: "forever"
855	  stream_json_logs:
856	    max_age_days: 30
857	    max_total_bytes: 2_000_000_000
858	  worktrees: "workflow-completion"
859	
860	logging:
861	  database: ".yoke/yoke.db"
862	  session_logs_dir: ".yoke/logs"
863	  retain_stream_json: true
864	
865	runtime:
866	  keep_awake: false   # when true, spawn caffeinate / systemd-inhibit as workflow child [D60]
867	
868	rate_limit:
869	  handling: "passive"     # v1: passive auto-retry after window reset
870	  # v1.1: add `usage_pause_threshold: 0.9` for proactive workflow-wide pause
871	
872	ui:
873	  port: 3456
874	  bind: "127.0.0.1"
875	  auth: false   # localhost single-user, always [D57]
876	```
877	
878	### Prompt template engine [D11]
879	
880	Mustache-style `{{name}}` replacer, hand-rolled minimal. No code execution. Undefined variable → hard error at assembly time with variable name + template path. Templates are pure — no file I/O, no shell. `PromptContext` is built by the Pipeline Engine from Worktree Manager + State Store + git helper and passed in as a plain object.
881	
882	Standard variables available to phases:
883	- `feature_spec`, `feature_id`, `workflow_name`, `architecture_md`, `progress_md`, `handoff_entries`, `git_log_recent`, `recent_diff`, `user_injected_context`
884	
885	Bootstrap `commands` and similar shell-exec'd strings do **not** interpolate variables in v1 (eliminates injection vector). Run with `spawn(cmd, args, {shell: false})`.
886	
887	---
888	
889	## Web Dashboard
890	
891	See protocol and render model above. Draft3 concrete specs:
892	
893	- **Workflow list** — paginated (`GET /api/workflows?status=&q=&before=&limit=`), keyset on `created_at`, filter bar, archive toggle, star pin. [D47]
894	- **Feature board** — category groups, status chips, fuzzy search, deep links, sticky currently-streaming pin, j/k nav. [D44]
895	- **Live streaming pane** — virtualized, follow-tail, normalized render model, system notices interleaved. [D41, D18]
896	- **Review panel** — Task tool_use specialization: collapsible subagent rows, per-angle status strip, subagent runs shown sequentially. [D46]
897	- **Manual controls** — matrix-driven, commandId idempotency. [D42]
898	- **GitHub buttons** — full state enum. [D48]
899	- **Crash recovery banner** — explicit `recoveryState`, user acknowledges. [D27]
900	- **Browser push** — service worker, one-gesture permission grant, fallback toast + bell on deny. [D45]
901	- **macOS native notifications** — server-side via node-notifier, deep-link URL back to dashboard. [D45]
902	- **Attention banner** — pulls from `pending_attention` table; never cleared by push failure. [D49]
903	
904	---
905	
906	## Usage Tracking
907	
908	See §Protocol for parsing rules. Stored per-session as columns (not blob). Aggregation endpoints:
909	
910	- `GET /api/workflows/:id/usage?groupBy=feature|phase|profile|session`
911	- `GET /api/workflows/:id/usage/timeseries?bucket=hour|day`
912	
913	Trends across workflows are v1.1.
914	
915	---
916	
917	## Review Architecture [D14]
918	
919	Orchestrator session is a thin spawner. In the orchestrator prompt, the only allowed tool is `Task` (to spawn subagents). Each subagent:
920	
921	1. Is scoped to Read, Bash, Glob, Grep + **Write** (scoped to `reviews/feature-N/<angle>.json` only). Scoping mechanism is whatever the user has wired in their command — jig profile is the recommended path in docs, Claude tool permissions or a PreToolUse deny hook work too.
922	2. Receives feature spec, acceptance criteria, review criteria, diff, architecture.md, handoff.json.
923	3. Writes its verdict **directly** to its own file.
924	4. Orchestrator never receives the verdict content.
925	
926	Harness aggregates after orchestrator exits:
927	- Parse each JSON in `reviews/feature-N/` against schema.
928	- Any parse failure → `review_invalid`, re-run once.
929	- Every criterion must have a verdict → enforced by schema.
930	- Final verdict = `any(fail) → fail`.
931	
932	---
933	
934	## Threat Model [D37]
935	
936	**Adversaries considered:**
937	1. **Prompt injection via spec.** Spec content is untrusted (may be pasted from Slack, GitHub issue, etc.). A malicious spec can instruct the planner to emit an `init.sh` that exfiltrates secrets.
938	2. **Confused-deputy agent.** A well-meaning but misaligned agent attempts actions outside its scope.
939	3. **Hook tampering.** Agent with Edit+Bash can rewrite the hook script to always pass.
940	
941	**Trust boundary:** the developer's machine. Yoke's job is to give users the tools to prevent a workflow from escaping the worktree and touching user files, and to give users the tools to prevent silent quality-gate bypass. **Enforcement is opt-in** — users wire it through their own Claude hooks or through Yoke `post:` commands.
942	
943	**v1 mitigations Yoke provides:**
944	- **PreToolUse safety template** (opt-in install at `yoke init`) — denies writes outside `worktreePath`, denies reads of `~/.ssh`, `~/.aws`, `~/.config/gh`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.gnupg`, curated Bash deny-list (`curl ... | sh`, `wget ... | sh`, `rm -rf /`, `chmod -R 777`, writes to `/etc/`). The template is a file the user copies into their Claude hook directory — Yoke does not install or manage it post-init.
945	- **Config flag `safety_mode: strict | default | yolo`** — controls which variant of the example template `yoke init` offers. `strict` adds a network egress allowlist and denies Bash `sudo`. `yolo` scaffolds no safety template at all. The flag is advisory — it parameterizes the template, not a runtime-enforced harness behavior.
946	- **`init.sh` first-run approval** — any `init.sh` generated by a planner requires explicit user approval before execution.
947	- **Review anti-skip heuristic** — reviewer checks tests were touched alongside production files; `post:` commands let the user enforce additional assertions.
948	
949	**v1 non-goals:**
950	- Sandboxing (firejail / sandbox-exec / Docker) — deferred to v2. [D51]
951	- Runtime-enforced allowlist or deny-list beyond what the user's own hooks do.
952	- Harness-managed hook integrity. If the user wants tamper detection, they add a `post:` command that checksums their hook files.
953	
954	---
955	
956	## Failure Modes [D35]
957	
958	Every failure below has a test fixture in the v1 suite.
959	
960	| Failure | Detection | User outcome | Recovery |
961	|---|---|---|---|
962	| configured command not found (ENOENT) | spawn error | clear error at workflow start naming the phase + command | fix config / PATH |
963	| configured command nonzero at start (e.g. jig profile missing) | early exit + stderr | phase fails with command + stderr snippet | fix user setup |
964	| worktree create fails (disk full) | git error | workflow start fails | free disk |
965	| worktree create fails (base branch missing) | git error | workflow start fails | fix base |
966	| bootstrap command fails | phase = bootstrap_failed | terminal state; no auto-cleanup | user inspects |
967	| SQLite locked | retry window; then fatal | attention banner | diagnose |
968	| SQLite corrupt on startup | integrity check | startup refuses | restore from backup; migration |
969	| rate limit mid-stream | stream-json event | `rate_limited` state, backoff | auto-resume after window |
970	| OAuth expired mid-stream | stream-json error | `awaiting_user`, notification | user refreshes auth |
971	| process killed externally | exit code | `awaiting_user` | user decides |
972	| laptop sleep / wake | stale PID on probe | session marked cancelled | user resumes |
973	| GitHub API down (auto-PR) | octokit error | button shows `failed`, manual fallback | retry or manual |
974	| gh unauthenticated | gh exit code | error with auth guidance | `gh auth login` |
975	| prompt template missing | load-time | workflow start fails | fix path |
976	| template variable undefined | assembly-time | phase fails with variable name | fix template |
977	| features.json schema mismatch | ajv | phase fails with path+message | fix spec |
978	| branch name collision | shouldn't happen (shortid suffix) | hard fail | bug |
979	| `pre:` command fails | nonzero exit | action per declared mapping (default: fail fast) | fix env / user action |
980	| `post:` command fails | nonzero exit | action per declared mapping | per user mapping |
981	| `post:` command hangs | 15 min timeout | killed + reported | diagnose |
982	| user hook hangs (if installed) | wall-clock ceiling | session killed | diagnose |
983	| stream-json parse error | per-line | counter bumps, skip line | inspect log |
984	| line > 16 MB | per-line | truncate, tainted flag | inspect log |
985	| disk full mid-write | WAL + write retry | tainted session, attention | free disk |
986	| stale inject-context | session consumed it | shown as consumed | n/a |
987	| review output missing criterion | schema reject | `review_invalid` → re-run | automatic |
988	| keep_awake helper dies unexpectedly | child exit | warning; workflow continues | automatic respawn attempt |
989	
990	---
991	
992	## Testability [D32, D33]
993	
994	### ProcessManager interface
995	
996	```ts
997	interface ProcessManager {
998	  spawn(opts: SpawnOpts): Promise<SpawnHandle>;
999	}
1000	
1001	class JigProcessManager implements ProcessManager { /* production */ }
1002	class ScriptedProcessManager implements ProcessManager {
1003	  /* replays recorded stream-json JSONL with configurable timing,
1004	     injected exit codes, injected stderr */
1005	}
1006	```
1007	
1008	### `yoke record` mode
1009	
1010	Captures a real session's stream-json + artifact file deltas into a fixture directory. Fixtures live in `tests/fixtures/<scenario>/`.
1011	
1012	### Fixture coverage required for v1
1013	
1014	At minimum one fixture per phase + at least one for each row in the Failure Modes table:
1015	- `plan-happy-path`
1016	- `implement-happy-path`
1017	- `review-happy-path`
1018	- `implement-hook-fail-then-retry`
1019	- `implement-rate-limit-midstream`
1020	- `implement-sigkill-midstream`
1021	- `review-missing-criterion` (→ review_invalid)
1022	- `malformed-stream-json-recovery`
1023	- `bootstrap-fails`
1024	- `hook-manifest-missing`
1025	- `hook-checksum-tampered`
1026	
1027	### FaultInjector
1028	
1029	Checkpoints: `before_persist`, `after_persist_before_spawn`, `after_spawn_before_ack`, `during_artifact_read`, `during_hook_exec`, `during_cleanup`.
1030	
1031	Actions per checkpoint: `panic | exit | kill-9-child | corrupt-next-write`.
1032	
1033	Tests assert recovery converges to the same terminal state as an uninterrupted run.
1034	
1035	---
1036	
1037	## v1 Acceptance (Release Gate) [D36]
1038	
1039	v1 is done when **all** of the following pass in CI against `ScriptedProcessManager` fixtures:
1040	
1041	1. Scripted happy-path plan → implement → review → complete in < 5 s.
1042	2. SIGTERM mid-implement → restart → workflow converges to same terminal state as uninterrupted run.
1043	3. `post:` command failing → declared action applied (retry / fail / goto) and observed behavior matches.
1044	4. `post:` command with `goto: <phase>` and `max_revisits: N` — revisit runs N times, then collapses to `awaiting_user`.
1045	5. Configured command ENOENT → clean error at workflow start naming the phase and the missing binary.
1046	6. Malformed features.json → schema error with path and message.
1047	7. Worktree creation failure → clear error, workflow stays in pre-bootstrap state.
1048	8. SQLite WAL crash during write → recovered on startup, no state loss.
1049	9. `blocked` feature can be manually retried from UI with injected context.
1050	10. Rate-limit fixture → `rate_limited` state → auto-resume after window → completion.
1051	11. Review with missing criterion → `review_invalid` → re-run → pass.
1052	12. Dashboard reconnect mid-stream → backfills from session log with no dupes, no gaps.
1053	13. Dashboard manual control (pause/resume/cancel) applies exactly once per `commandId`.
1054	14. `yoke doctor` reports dangling processes after simulated hard crash.
1055	15. `keep_awake: true` fixture → platform helper spawned, dies with workflow.
1056	16. Parallel workflows — **deferred to v1.1** per D52. Not part of v1 release gate.
1057	
1058	Release gate = all green. Acceptance runs are recorded in `docs/releases/v1-acceptance-<timestamp>.md`.
1059	
1060	---
1061	
1062	## What Yoke Does NOT Do
1063	
1064	- Not rate-limit management in v1 (passive auto-retry only; proactive pause is v1.1).
1065	- Not an IDE. Dashboard monitors and controls; does not edit code.
1066	- Not LLM-agnostic. Claude-Code-native by design.
1067	- Not jig-dependent. `jig` is a recommended docs-level layer, never a runtime requirement. [D55]
1068	- Not a CI/CD system.
1069	- Not timeout-based (except: command wall-clock ceilings on hooks / `pre:` / `post:`, explicitly justified).
1070	- Not a token optimizer.
1071	- Not a hook installer / manager. Ships example templates; user owns their hook directory. [D55]
1072	- **Not multi-user, ever.** Localhost single-user forever. Multiple Yoke instances of the same user are fine. [D57]
1073	- **Not parallel-workflow in v1.** Single workflow at a time per instance. [D52]
1074	
1075	---
1076	
1077	## Requirements
1078	
1079	### Must Have (v1)
1080	
1081	- [ ] Pipeline engine reading `.yoke.yml`, phase graph (not just list)
1082	- [ ] State machine with explicit transition table + unit-tested coverage
1083	- [ ] Failure classifier (transient/permanent/policy/unknown) + exponential backoff
1084	- [ ] Phase definitions with `command` / `args` (jig-agnostic), prompt template, output artifacts, success criteria
1085	- [ ] **Phase `pre:` and `post:` command arrays** with exit-code action mapping, `max_revisits` loop guards, session-log capture [D50, D53]
1086	- [ ] Prompt Assembler (pure fn) + Mustache-style template engine + `PromptContext` builder
1087	- [ ] Process Manager with process-group isolation, SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, NDJSON line-buffered stream-json parser, EPIPE handling — **command-agnostic** (jig optional)
1088	- [ ] Two-signal heartbeat (liveness + stream-activity), warnings only, never auto-kill
1089	- [ ] Worktree Manager (create, bootstrap as a phase, teardown hook, cleanup)
1090	- [ ] SQLite store with WAL, migrations, indexes, transactions, `events` + `artifact_writes` + `pending_attention` tables
1091	- [ ] Artifact validators (ajv) for features.json and review files
1092	- [ ] `handoff.json` as structured cross-phase channel
1093	- [ ] `.yoke/status-updates.jsonl` narrow status channel from implementer
1094	- [ ] features.json post-phase diff check (harness enforcement)
1095	- [ ] Topological sort of `depends_on` + cascade-blocking
1096	- [ ] Retry ladder (`continue` → `fresh_with_failure_summary` → `awaiting_user`) + configurable `on_review_fail` [D56]
1097	- [ ] Crash recovery: fresh-session reconstruction; `-c` used only as soft optimization; dirty-worktree auto-stash
1098	- [ ] `.yoke/last-check.json` **optional manifest display** in dashboard (user's choice to emit) [D55]
1099	- [ ] Passive rate-limit handling: detect, enter `rate_limited`, wait for window, auto-resume [D61]
1100	- [ ] `keep_awake` opt-in: platform-specific idle-inhibit child spawned with workflow [D60]
1101	- [ ] `yoke init`: opt-in scaffolding of example hook templates + example `post:` command set; never overwrites existing files
1102	- [ ] Threat model doc + `safety_mode: strict/default/yolo` parameterizing example templates
1103	- [ ] Fastify HTTP + ws server with protocol envelope (v1 types, per-session `seq`, `hello`, subscribe/unsubscribe, backfill)
1104	- [ ] Session Log Store (`.yoke/logs/`) with HTTP paging endpoint
1105	- [ ] Structured log schema + `GET /api/workflows/:id/timeline`
1106	- [ ] Token usage as columns + `parent_session_id` chaining
1107	- [ ] Web dashboard: workflow list (paginated), feature board (grouped/filtered/searchable/nav), live streaming (virtualized + normalized render model), system notices interleaved, review fan-out rendering, crash recovery banner, manual controls (matrix + commandId), GitHub buttons (full state enum), attention banner, pre/post command output rendering
1108	- [ ] Service-worker browser push + macOS native with deep-links
1109	- [ ] Notifications severity classes + `pending_attention` table as authoritative
1110	- [ ] Review orchestrator pattern: subagents write directly, harness aggregates
1111	- [ ] `ScriptedProcessManager` + fixtures (one per failure mode) + `yoke record` mode
1112	- [ ] `FaultInjector` seam + crash-recovery test suite
1113	- [ ] Failure Modes table entries each covered by a fixture
1114	- [ ] v1 Acceptance scenarios passing in CI
1115	- [ ] CLI: `yoke init`, `yoke start`, `yoke status`, `yoke cancel`, `yoke doctor`
1116	- [ ] Default prompt templates for plan / implement / review
1117	- [ ] README, config guide, threat model doc, prompt template guide, **hook best-practices guide (mentions jig as recommended)**
1118	- [ ] `version: "1"` schema pin + helpful ajv error messages
1119	
1120	### Should Have (v1.1)
1121	
1122	- [ ] Parallel workflows (concurrency contract, shared resource isolation, stress test) [D52]
1123	- [ ] Proactive rate-limit pause: `usage_pause_threshold` workflow-wide percent; harness signals session to wrap up via handoff.json and pauses workflow [D61]
1124	- [ ] Iterative planning UX polish (structural affordance already in v1 via `post:` + `goto`)
1125	- [ ] Optional pre-step usage gate
1126	- [ ] GitHub artifact attachment + issue linking
1127	- [ ] Token usage reporting (per-feature/phase/profile) UI
1128	- [ ] Searchable log viewer UI (storage is v1)
1129	- [ ] Webhook notifications (Slack, Discord)
1130	- [ ] Workflow report export
1131	- [ ] Windows `keep_awake` implementation (macOS + Linux land in v1)
1132	
1133	### Nice to Have (v2)
1134	
1135	- [ ] Sandboxed execution (firejail / sandbox-exec / Docker)
1136	- [ ] Active rate-limit polling (status-line or ephemeral probe sessions) [D61]
1137	- [ ] Log analysis + failure pattern detection
1138	- [ ] Automatic CLAUDE.md / prompt template suggestions
1139	- [ ] Plugin system for custom validators between phases
1140	- [ ] GitHub issue → workflow automation
1141	- [ ] Electron wrapper
1142	
1143	*Note: multi-user is never on the roadmap. Yoke is and stays single-user-per-instance. [D57]*
1144	
1145	---
1146	
1147	## Tech Stack
1148	
1149	Unchanged from draft2 except:
1150	- Add: `@tanstack/react-virtual`
1151	- Add: hand-rolled Mustache-style template engine (or `mustache` pinned)
1152	- Add: `ajv-errors` or custom error formatter
1153	- Confirm: `better-sqlite3`, `fastify`, `ws`, `simple-git`, `node-notifier`, `octokit`, `commander`, `yaml`, `ajv`
1154	
1155	---
1156	
1157	## Build Order
1158	
1159	Aligns with the dogfooding runbook (see `runbook.md`). Every bullet is an agent task driven either manually (Tier 0), via `yoke-v0` shell glue (Tier 0.5), or via v1 itself once the pipeline engine exists.
1160	
1161	### Phase β — Core Design (Architect, Tier 0)
1162	Architect produces `docs/design/`: module boundaries, schemas, API contracts, prompt-template spec, open-questions resolution, state machine transition table.
1163	
1164	### Phase γ — Empirical Research + `yoke-v0` Bootstrap (Backend, Tier 0 → 0.5)
1165	1. Research tasks capturing real Claude Code behavior (stream-json framing, `-c` semantics, Claude hook exit codes / stdin / stdout schema, token usage events, rate-limit stream-json frames) → `docs/research/*.md`.
1166	2. Minimal `yoke-v0` shell script: template assembly + command-agnostic spawn (`claude` by default, or whatever the user's config names) + stream-json capture to `.yoke/logs/`.
1167	
1168	### Phase δ — Core Engine (Backend, Tier 0.5)
1169	Use `yoke-v0` to drive implementation of: config parser, SQLite store + migrations, pipeline engine + transition table, process manager (command-agnostic + Scripted), pre/post command runner + action grammar, worktree manager, optional-manifest reader, artifact validators, CLI, Session Log Store, fastify + ws skeleton with protocol envelope, passive rate-limit handling, `keep_awake` helper.
1170	
1171	By end of δ, v1 self-hosts and graduates to Tier 1.
1172	
1173	### Phase ε — Dashboard + Integrations (Frontend, Tier 1)
1174	React app, normalized render model, virtualized live pane, control matrix, crash recovery banner, browser push + macOS native, GitHub buttons, attention banner, workflow history.
1175	
1176	### Phase ζ — QA + Release (QA, Tier 1)
1177	Failure Modes fixtures, FaultInjector tests, v1 Acceptance scenarios running in CI, docs pass, release.
1178	
1179	---
1180	
1181	## Resolved decisions (summary)
1182	
1183	All open items from Phase α.5 are resolved. Full decision text lives in `change-log.md` §Q. Headline outcomes:
1184	
1185	- **D50** Configurable `pre:` / `post:` shell commands per phase with exit-code action grammar (replaces polyglot runner).
1186	- **D51** Sandboxing deferred to v2. `safety_mode` parameterizes example templates only.
1187	- **D52** v1 is single-workflow. Parallel workflows in v1.1.
1188	- **D53** Phase transitions via condition commands (`continue`, `goto`, `goto-offset`, `retry`, `stop-and-ask`, `stop`, `fail`) with `max_revisits` loop guards. Replaces hardcoded iterative-planning affordance.
1189	- **D54** `architecture.md` is both an optional user-supplied input and a planner output (planner proposes edits via `architecture-proposed.md`).
1190	- **D55** Jig is optional docs-only. Claude hooks live in Claude's namespace, owned by the user. Yoke ships example templates, no hook installation/management. Quality gating is the user's choice of Claude Stop hook and/or Yoke `post:` commands.
1191	- **D56** Review-fail re-implement defaults to fresh, configurable via `on_review_fail: { retry_mode }`.
1192	- **D57** Single-user forever. Dashboard binds 127.0.0.1. No auth, ever. Multiple Yoke instances of the same user are supported.
1193	- **D58** SQLite forever, stream-json logs 30d/2GB, worktrees on completion. All configurable. Remote log forwarding is the user's concern.
1194	- **D59** Workflow continues non-dependent features when one blocks; cascade-blocks dependents; terminal state "completed with blocked features."
1195	- **D60** Laptop-primary. Opt-in `keep_awake: true` spawns `caffeinate -i` (macOS) / `systemd-inhibit` (Linux) as workflow child. Windows v1.1.
1196	- **D61** Passive rate-limit handling in v1: enter `rate_limited`, wait for window, auto-resume. Proactive workflow-wide pause at configurable % threshold in v1.1. Active polling v2.
1197

summary: Read key design docs for pipeline engine

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have both complete files. Here are the full contents:

## File 1: `/Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/docs/idea/plan-draft3.md`

This is Yoke's comprehensive Plan Draft 3 specification. It supersedes Draft 2 and incorporates all 61 decisions (D01–D61) from the change log. The document is 1196 lines and covers:

**Core sections include:**
- Vision: Claude-Code-native orchestration harness for long-running autonomous software development
- Architecture: Pipeline Engine, Process Manager, Worktree Manager, Session Log Store, SQLite, all coordinated by the harness
- Core Design Principles: 8 fundamental principles including file-based agent communication, optional quality gates, SQLite as single source of truth
- Workflow Lifecycle: Plan → Implement → Review → Completion phases
- Phase Pre/Post Commands: Configurable shell commands with exit-code action grammar
- State Machine: 12 states with explicit transition table
- File Contract: features.json (read-only projection), status-updates.jsonl, handoff.json (structured cross-phase channel), reviews
- Protocol Layer: NDJSON stream-json parsing, WebSocket envelope with versioning and subscription model
- Client Render Model: Normalized event types (TextBlock, ToolCall, ThinkingBlock, SystemNotice, UsageUpdate)
- Hooks Integration: Jig optional, Claude hooks user-owned, example templates provided
- Worktree Management: Branch naming, bootstrap as own phase, teardown script, cleanup order
- Process Management: Command-agnostic spawning, SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, dual-signal heartbeat
- Crash Recovery: Fresh sessions use artifacts/git/handoff.json; `-c` only soft optimization
- SQLite Schema: WAL mode explicit, migrations forward-only, comprehensive table definitions
- Configuration: Full .yoke.yml spec with phases, worktrees, notifications, GitHub, retention, logging
- Web Dashboard: Workflow list, feature board, live streaming pane, review panel, manual controls, GitHub buttons, crash recovery banner, browser push
- Usage Tracking: Per-session columns, aggregation endpoints
- Review Architecture: Orchestrator spawns subagents sequentially; each writes verdict directly
- Threat Model: Prompt injection, confused-deputy, hook tampering; mitigations include PreToolUse deny hook, safety_mode flag, init.sh approval
- Failure Modes: 23 enumerated failure scenarios each with detection, outcome, recovery
- Testability: ProcessManager interface with JigProcessManager and ScriptedProcessManager, yoke record mode, FaultInjector seam with checkpoints
- v1 Acceptance: 16 concrete release-gate scenarios (parallel workflows deferred to v1.1)
- What Yoke Does NOT Do: Not IDE, not LLM-agnostic, not parallel-workflow in v1, not multi-user ever
- Requirements: Comprehensive Must-Have (v1), Should-Have (v1.1), and Nice-to-Have (v2) checklists
- Tech Stack: TypeScript/Node, @tanstack/react-virtual, better-sqlite3, fastify, ws, simple-git, node-notifier, octokit, yaml, ajv
- Build Order: Phases β–ζ aligned with dogfooding runbook (Architect → Research+Bootstrap → Core Engine → Dashboard → QA+Release)
- Resolved Decisions: Summary of D50–D61 outcomes

---

## File 2: `/Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/docs/idea/change-log.md`

This is the "Plan Draft 2 → Draft 3 Change Log" synthesizing four Phase α critiques (architect, backend, frontend, QA) against Draft 2. It is 403 lines and documents:

**Sections (A–R):**
- **A. Core principles & architectural honesty (D01–D04):** Drop adapter-ready escape hatch; rewrite "token usage" principle; collapse State Store into SQLite; split Artifact Store into Worktree Artifacts and Session Log Store
- **B. State machine & phase model (D05–D09):** Decouple state machine from phase names; add explicit transition table; add failure classification; implement topological sort + cascade-blocking; structural affordance for iterative planning
- **C. File-artifact integrity (D10–D13):** features.json as read-only projection; define PromptContext for Prompt Assembler; add session/artifact provenance; introduce handoff.json as structured cross-phase channel
- **D. Review architecture (D14):** Subagents write directly to reviews/feature-N/<angle>.json; harness aggregates deterministically
- **E. Protocol layer (D15–D20):** Commit to NDJSON; specify WebSocket envelope with versioning; define backfill-on-reconnect; add subscription model with 4-concurrent cap
- **F. Process lifecycle (D21–D23):** Single stdin.end() call with EPIPE handler; SIGTERM→SIGKILL with 10s wait and process-group isolation; two-signal heartbeat (liveness + stream-activity)
- **G. Retry + recovery (D24–D27):** Separate outer retries from inner hook retries; define retry-mode ladder; clarify `-c` as soft optimization; auto-stash dirty worktrees
- **H. Hooks & quality gates (D28–D31):** .yoke/last-check.json manifest contract; Stop hook exit codes to verify empirically; checksum validation; configurable timeout
- **I. Testability (D32–D33):** ProcessManager interface with Scripted implementation; FaultInjector with named checkpoints
- **J. Observability & failure enumeration (D34–D36):** Structured log schema with correlation; comprehensive Failure Modes table; v1 Acceptance scenarios as release gate
- **K. Security & threat model (D37):** PreToolUse deny hook template; safety_mode config; init.sh approval
- **L. SQLite & persistence (D38–D39):** Migrations, indexes, explicit WAL, transactions; token usage as columns
- **M. Worktree management (D40):** Worktree suffixed by workflow id; bootstrap as own phase; teardown hook; cleanup order; unpushed-commit guard
- **N. Frontend / rendering (D41–D48a):** Virtualization with @tanstack/react-virtual; control matrix; inject-context state; feature board scaling; GitHub state enum; Session log storage promoted to v1
- **O. Notifications (D49):** severity classification with fire-and-forget info vs. retried requires_attention
- **P. Scope movements (v1 ↔ v1.1):** Table showing 14 items moved between versions (e.g., topo sort, structured logs, handoff.json moved to v1; parallel workflows, proactive rate-limit pause stay v1.1)
- **Q. Resolved decisions (D50–D61):** 12 originally-open items resolved with some reframed:
  - D50: pre:/post: command arrays with exit-code action grammar
  - D51: Sandboxing deferred to v2
  - D52: Single-workflow v1, parallel v1.1
  - D53: Phase transitions via condition commands
  - D54: architecture.md as input+output (proposes via architecture-proposed.md)
  - D55: Jig optional, hooks user-owned, example templates provided
  - D56: Review-fail defaults fresh, configurable
  - D57: Single-user forever, 127.0.0.1 only
  - D58: SQLite forever, logs 30d/2GB, worktrees on completion
  - D59: Non-dependent features continue when one blocks
  - D60: Opt-in keep_awake with platform-specific idle inhibit
  - D61: Passive rate-limit v1 (enter state + auto-resume), proactive pause v1.1
- **R. Phase γ empirical corrections (R01–R03):** Notes from hook-semantics research that affect only docs/templates scope:
  - Hooks in .claude/settings.json not .claude/hooks/ directory
  - Default hook timeout 600s not 15m
  - stop_hook_active boolean replaces counter file

**Dispositions:** All 61 decisions accepted; 56 resolved as-is, 5 reframed (D50, D53, D55, D61 plus R03).

---

Both files are essential architectural and decision documentation for the Yoke project, establishing the complete specification and rationale for v1 development.

## File 1: `/Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/docs/idea/plan-draft3.md`

This is Yoke's comprehensive Plan Draft 3 specification. It supersedes Draft 2 and incorporates all 61 decisions (D01–D61) from the change log. The document is 1196 lines and covers:

**Core sections include:**
- Vision: Claude-Code-native orchestration harness for long-running autonomous software development
- Architecture: Pipeline Engine, Process Manager, Worktree Manager, Session Log Store, SQLite, all coordinated by the harness
- Core Design Principles: 8 fundamental principles including file-based agent communication, optional quality gates, SQLite as single source of truth
- Workflow Lifecycle: Plan → Implement → Review → Completion phases
- Phase Pre/Post Commands: Configurable shell commands with exit-code action grammar
- State Machine: 12 states with explicit transition table
- File Contract: features.json (read-only projection), status-updates.jsonl, handoff.json (structured cross-phase channel), reviews
- Protocol Layer: NDJSON stream-json parsing, WebSocket envelope with versioning and subscription model
- Client Render Model: Normalized event types (TextBlock, ToolCall, ThinkingBlock, SystemNotice, UsageUpdate)
- Hooks Integration: Jig optional, Claude hooks user-owned, example templates provided
- Worktree Management: Branch naming, bootstrap as own phase, teardown script, cleanup order
- Process Management: Command-agnostic spawning, SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, dual-signal heartbeat
- Crash Recovery: Fresh sessions use artifacts/git/handoff.json; `-c` only soft optimization
- SQLite Schema: WAL mode explicit, migrations forward-only, comprehensive table definitions
- Configuration: Full .yoke.yml spec with phases, worktrees, notifications, GitHub, retention, logging
- Web Dashboard: Workflow list, feature board, live streaming pane, review panel, manual controls, GitHub buttons, crash recovery banner, browser push
- Usage Tracking: Per-session columns, aggregation endpoints
- Review Architecture: Orchestrator spawns subagents sequentially; each writes verdict directly
- Threat Model: Prompt injection, confused-deputy, hook tampering; mitigations include PreToolUse deny hook, safety_mode flag, init.sh approval
- Failure Modes: 23 enumerated failure scenarios each with detection, outcome, recovery
- Testability: ProcessManager interface with JigProcessManager and ScriptedProcessManager, yoke record mode, FaultInjector seam with checkpoints
- v1 Acceptance: 16 concrete release-gate scenarios (parallel workflows deferred to v1.1)
- What Yoke Does NOT Do: Not IDE, not LLM-agnostic, not parallel-workflow in v1, not multi-user ever
- Requirements: Comprehensive Must-Have (v1), Should-Have (v1.1), and Nice-to-Have (v2) checklists
- Tech Stack: TypeScript/Node, @tanstack/react-virtual, better-sqlite3, fastify, ws, simple-git, node-notifier, octokit, yaml, ajv
- Build Order: Phases β–ζ aligned with dogfooding runbook (Architect → Research+Bootstrap → Core Engine → Dashboard → QA+Release)
- Resolved Decisions: Summary of D50–D61 outcomes

---
