1	-- Yoke — SQLite schema (final DDL)
2	-- Source: plan-draft3.md §SQLite Schema (D38), §File Contract (D10, D12),
3	-- §Crash Recovery (D27), §Phase Pre/Post Commands (D50).
4	-- Revised per beta3-review-feedback.md Issues 1–4: features→items rename,
5	-- opaque data column, stage tracking, review aggregation removed.
6	--
7	-- Discipline:
8	--   * single writer (better-sqlite3), readers use a separate connection
9	--   * every state transition wrapped in db.transaction(fn)()
10	--   * WAL mode, normal sync, foreign keys on
11	--   * forward-only migrations; this file represents the target state,
12	--     individual migration files live under src/server/storage/migrations/
13	
14	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	-- PRAGMAs (set on every connection open, both writer and reader)
16	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
18	PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;
19	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
20	-- Recommended for long-running orchestrator:
21	--   busy_timeout lets readers wait briefly instead of instant SQLITE_BUSY.
22	PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;
23	-- WAL autocheckpoint is fine at default; document for ops in retention config.
24	
25	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	-- Migrations bootstrap
27	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
29	  version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
30	  applied_at TEXT NOT NULL
31	);
32	
33	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	-- workflows — one row per named workflow run.
35	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	CREATE TABLE workflows (
37	  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
38	  name            TEXT NOT NULL,
39	  spec            TEXT NOT NULL,      -- the user-provided spec (text/markdown)
40	  pipeline        TEXT NOT NULL,      -- JSON: resolved stage list (Issue 1)
41	  config          TEXT NOT NULL,      -- JSON: resolved .yoke.yml
42	  status          TEXT NOT NULL,      -- matches state-machine state labels
43	  current_stage   TEXT,               -- stage id currently executing (Issue 1)
44	  worktree_path   TEXT,
45	  branch_name     TEXT,
46	  recovery_state  TEXT,               -- JSON; set on restart, cleared on ack
47	  created_at      TEXT NOT NULL,
48	  updated_at      TEXT NOT NULL
49	);
50	CREATE INDEX idx_workflows_status ON workflows(status);
51	CREATE INDEX idx_workflows_created_at ON workflows(created_at DESC);
52	
53	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	-- items — canonical item store. Item data is opaque to the harness (Issue 2).
55	-- Harness state is separated from user data (Issue 3).
56	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	CREATE TABLE items (
58	  id                          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
59	  workflow_id                 TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
60	  stage_id                    TEXT NOT NULL,   -- stage this item belongs to (Issue 1)
61	  data                        TEXT NOT NULL,   -- JSON: opaque user data blob (Issue 2, Issue 3)
62	  status                      TEXT NOT NULL,   -- state-machine label (harness state, Issue 3)
63	  current_phase               TEXT,            -- free-text (D05, harness state)
64	  depends_on                  TEXT,            -- JSON array of item ids; extracted via items_depends_on (D08)
65	  retry_count                 INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
66	  retry_window_start          TEXT,            -- for exponential-backoff window (D07)
67	  created_by_session_id       TEXT,            -- provenance (D12, harness state)
68	  last_updated_by_session_id  TEXT,
69	  blocked_reason              TEXT,
70	  updated_at                  TEXT NOT NULL
71	);
72	CREATE INDEX idx_items_workflow ON items(workflow_id);
73	CREATE INDEX idx_items_status ON items(workflow_id, status);
74	CREATE INDEX idx_items_stage ON items(workflow_id, stage_id);
75	
76	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	-- sessions — one row per spawned agent session (plan, implement, review, ...).
78	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	CREATE TABLE sessions (
80	  id                          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
81	  workflow_id                 TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
82	  item_id                     TEXT REFERENCES items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
83	  parent_session_id           TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id),
84	  -- parent_session_id exists to chain -c continuation sessions so that
85	  -- per-session usage is the delta from a single spawn and per-feature
86	  -- usage sums the chain (plan-draft3 §stream-json parsing, D16).
87	  stage                       TEXT NOT NULL,           -- stage id (Issue 1)
88	  phase                       TEXT NOT NULL,           -- free-text label
89	  agent_profile               TEXT NOT NULL,
90	  pid                         INTEGER,
91	  pgid                        INTEGER,
92	  started_at                  TEXT NOT NULL,
93	  ended_at                    TEXT,
94	  exit_code                   INTEGER,
95	  -- Token usage as columns (D39) plus raw_usage as an opaque escape hatch
96	  -- for any extra fields that arrive on a Phase γ verification pass.
97	  input_tokens                INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
98	  output_tokens               INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
99	  cache_creation_input_tokens INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
100	  cache_read_input_tokens     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
101	  raw_usage                   TEXT,
102	  session_log_path            TEXT,             -- ~/.yoke/<fingerprint>/logs/<wf>/<id>.jsonl
103	  status                      TEXT NOT NULL,    -- running|ok|fail|cancelled|tainted
104	  status_flags                TEXT,             -- JSON: {parse_errors, tainted, ...}
105	  last_event_at               TEXT,
106	  last_event_type             TEXT
107	);
108	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_workflow ON sessions(workflow_id, item_id);
109	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_item ON sessions(item_id);
110	CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_parent ON sessions(parent_session_id);
111	
112	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	-- events — append-only state-machine + harness debug trace (D38).
114	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
115	CREATE TABLE events (
116	  id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
117	  ts          TEXT NOT NULL,
118	  workflow_id TEXT REFERENCES workflows(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
119	  item_id     TEXT REFERENCES items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
120	  session_id  TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
121	  stage       TEXT,
122	  phase       TEXT,
123	  attempt     INTEGER,
124	  event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,
125	  level       TEXT NOT NULL,   -- debug|info|warn|error
126	  message     TEXT NOT NULL,
127	  extra       TEXT             -- JSON
128	);
129	CREATE INDEX idx_events_workflow ON events(workflow_id, ts);
130	CREATE INDEX idx_events_session ON events(session_id, ts);
131	CREATE INDEX idx_events_type ON events(event_type);
132	
133	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	-- artifact_writes — provenance trail for every file written by a session.
135	-- Populated by the Process Manager on session end via a worktree diff scan
136	-- and by the Pre/Post Runner for command-produced files.
137	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	CREATE TABLE artifact_writes (
139	  id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
140	  session_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
141	  artifact_path TEXT NOT NULL,
142	  written_at    TEXT NOT NULL,
143	  sha256        TEXT NOT NULL
144	);
145	CREATE INDEX idx_artifact_writes_session ON artifact_writes(session_id);
146	CREATE INDEX idx_artifact_writes_path ON artifact_writes(artifact_path);
147	
148	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
149	-- pending_attention — authoritative source for the dashboard attention banner
150	-- (D49). Populated when a workflow enters awaiting_user / blocked / crash
151	-- recovery / rate_limited-with-no-resume. Cleared on user acknowledgement.
152	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	CREATE TABLE pending_attention (
154	  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
155	  workflow_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
156	  kind            TEXT NOT NULL,
157	  -- kind examples: blocked_item, crash_recovery_required,
158	  --                rate_limited_long, stage_needs_approval,
159	  --                post_command_fail, awaiting_user_retry
160	  payload         TEXT NOT NULL,   -- JSON
161	  created_at      TEXT NOT NULL,
162	  acknowledged_at TEXT
163	);
164	CREATE INDEX idx_pending_attention_workflow ON pending_attention(workflow_id);
165	CREATE INDEX idx_pending_attention_open ON pending_attention(workflow_id) WHERE acknowledged_at IS NULL;
166	
167	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	-- prepost_runs — per-command execution record (D50). One row per command
169	-- per phase attempt. Not in plan-draft3 §SQLite Schema; added because
170	-- plan-draft3 §v1 Acceptance scenario 3 requires observable evidence
171	-- that a declared action was applied (Q-prepost-table, accepted).
172	-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
173	CREATE TABLE prepost_runs (
174	  id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
175	  session_id   TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
176	  workflow_id  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workflows(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
177	  item_id      TEXT REFERENCES items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
178	  stage        TEXT NOT NULL,
179	  phase        TEXT NOT NULL,
180	  when_phase   TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (when_phase IN ('pre', 'post')),
181	  command_name TEXT NOT NULL,
182	  argv         TEXT NOT NULL,   -- JSON array
183	  started_at   TEXT NOT NULL,
184	  ended_at     TEXT,
185	  exit_code    INTEGER,
186	  action_taken TEXT,            -- JSON: resolved action object
187	  stdout_path  TEXT,
188	  stderr_path  TEXT
189	);
190	CREATE INDEX idx_prepost_workflow ON prepost_runs(workflow_id, started_at);
191	CREATE INDEX idx_prepost_session ON prepost_runs(session_id);
192

1	/**
2	 * State Machine — TRANSITIONS const and transition() pure lookup function.
3	 *
4	 * Source of truth: docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md
5	 *
6	 * Every (State, Event) pair listed in that document has a row here.
7	 * An unknown pair — one not listed in the document — returns `undefined`
8	 * from `transition()`; it never throws.
9	 *
10	 * The Pipeline Engine is the sole consumer of this table.  It:
11	 *   1. Evaluates guards (using live SQLite / runtime context).
12	 *   2. Commits the SQLite transition inside db.transaction().
13	 *   3. Executes the side effects indicated by `sideEffects`.
14	 *
15	 * This module has NO side effects of its own.  It performs no SQLite
16	 * writes, no signal sends, no timer creation.  `transition()` is a
17	 * pure function: same inputs always produce the same TransitionResult.
18	 */
19	
20	import type { State, Event } from './states.js';
21	
22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	// Result types
24	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	
26	/**
27	 * A single guarded outcome in a conditional transition.
28	 * The Pipeline Engine picks the first outcome whose guard passes.
29	 */
30	export interface TransitionOutcome {
31	  /** Target state when this outcome is selected. */
32	  to: State;
33	  /**
34	   * Human-readable guard condition; enforcement is the Pipeline Engine's
35	   * responsibility.  The table documents it; the Engine enforces it.
36	   */
37	  guard: string;
38	  /**
39	   * Symbolic labels for side effects the Pipeline Engine executes after
40	   * committing the SQLite transition.  These are declarative labels, not
41	   * code — the Engine maps labels to concrete actions.
42	   */
43	  sideEffects: readonly string[];
44	}
45	
46	/**
47	 * Unconditional (single-outcome) transition result.
48	 */
49	export interface DirectTransitionResult {
50	  kind: 'direct';
51	  /** Target state. */
52	  to: State;
53	  /**
54	   * Guard condition if any; absent or empty means unconditional.
55	   * The Engine checks this before committing.
56	   */
57	  guard?: string;
58	  /** Symbolic side-effect labels. */
59	  sideEffects: readonly string[];
60	}
61	
62	/**
63	 * Conditional (multi-outcome) transition result.
64	 * The Pipeline Engine evaluates `outcomes` in order and applies the
65	 * first one whose guard passes.
66	 */
67	export interface ConditionalTransitionResult {
68	  kind: 'conditional';
69	  outcomes: readonly TransitionOutcome[];
70	}
71	
72	export type TransitionResult = DirectTransitionResult | ConditionalTransitionResult;
73	
74	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	// Helpers
76	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	function direct(
79	  to: State,
80	  sideEffects: readonly string[],
81	  guard?: string,
82	): DirectTransitionResult {
83	  return { kind: 'direct', to, sideEffects, ...(guard !== undefined ? { guard } : {}) };
84	}
85	
86	function conditional(outcomes: readonly TransitionOutcome[]): ConditionalTransitionResult {
87	  return { kind: 'conditional', outcomes };
88	}
89	
90	function outcome(
91	  to: State,
92	  guard: string,
93	  sideEffects: readonly string[] = [],
94	): TransitionOutcome {
95	  return { to, guard, sideEffects };
96	}
97	
98	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	// Shared result constants (reused across multiple rows)
100	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	
102	const ABANDONED_NOOP = direct('abandoned', []);
103	
104	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	// TRANSITIONS — the closed (State × Event) → TransitionResult map.
106	//
107	// Type { [S in State]: Partial<Record<Event, TransitionResult>> } enforces
108	// that every State is a key.  Adding a new State to the union type without
109	// adding the corresponding entry here produces a TypeScript compile error.
110	//
111	// Events absent from a state's row return `undefined` from transition();
112	// that is the correct "unknown pair" behaviour.
113	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	
115	export const TRANSITIONS: { [S in State]: Partial<Record<Event, TransitionResult>> } = {
116	
117	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	  // pending
119	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
120	  pending: {
121	    deps_satisfied: conditional([
122	      outcome(
123	        'ready',
124	        'all depends_on items have status in {complete}',
125	        ['enqueue item for scheduling'],
126	      ),
127	    ]),
128	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', ['cascade-cancel dependents']),
129	  },
130	
131	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	  // ready
133	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	  ready: {
135	    phase_start: conditional([
136	      outcome(
137	        'bootstrapping',
138	        'worktree_path null or absent',
139	        ['invoke Worktree Manager.create'],
140	      ),
141	      outcome(
142	        'in_progress',
143	        'worktree already exists',
144	        ['skip bootstrap, spawn agent'],
145	      ),
146	    ]),
147	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', []),
148	  },
149	
150	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	  // bootstrapping
152	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	  bootstrapping: {
154	    bootstrap_ok: direct(
155	      'in_progress',
156	      ['write worktree_path', 'write branch_name', 'emit phase_start'],
157	    ),
158	    bootstrap_fail: direct(
159	      'bootstrap_failed',
160	      ['insert pending_attention{kind=bootstrap_failed}'],
161	    ),
162	  },
163	
164	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
165	  // bootstrap_failed
166	  // Terminal until user_retry or user_cancel.
167	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	  bootstrap_failed: {
169	    user_retry: direct(
170	      'bootstrapping',
171	      ['re-run bootstrap'],
172	      'user action recorded',
173	    ),
174	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', ['run teardown', 'remove worktree']),
175	  },
176	
177	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	  // in_progress
179	  // All phase types (implement, review, user-defined) share this state.
180	  // current_phase distinguishes which phase is active.
181	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	  in_progress: {
183	    pre_command_failed: conditional([
184	      outcome(
185	        'awaiting_retry',
186	        'action=fail AND retry budget > 0',
187	        ['log to events', 'log to prepost_runs'],
188	      ),
189	      outcome(
190	        'awaiting_user',
191	        'action=fail AND retry budget exhausted',
192	        ['log to events', 'log to prepost_runs'],
193	      ),
194	      outcome(
195	        'awaiting_user',
196	        'action=stop-and-ask',
197	        ['insert pending_attention'],
198	      ),
199	    ]),
200	
201	    pre_commands_ok: direct('in_progress', ['spawn agent session']),
202	
203	    session_spawned: direct('in_progress', ['attach stream-json parser']),
204	
205	    rate_limit_detected: direct(
206	      'rate_limited',
207	      ['suppress heartbeat', 'schedule resume timer'],
208	    ),
209	
210	    // session_ok is a composite trigger: the Pipeline Engine accumulates
211	    // session_ok + validators_ok + diff_check_ok + all post_command_ok
212	    // before performing this lookup.  Non-continue post actions fire
213	    // post_command_action events (handled below) before session_ok.
214	    session_ok: conditional([
215	      outcome(
216	        'in_progress',
217	        'validators_ok AND diff_check_ok AND all_post_commands_ok AND more phases in stage',
218	        ['advance current_phase', 'emit phase_advance'],
219	      ),
220	      outcome(
221	        'complete',
222	        'validators_ok AND diff_check_ok AND all_post_commands_ok AND last phase in stage',
223	        ['check stage completion', 'emit deps_satisfied cascade'],
224	      ),
225	    ]),
226	
227	    // post_command_action dispatch (plan-draft3 §Phase Pre/Post Commands).
228	    post_command_action: conditional([
229	      outcome(
230	        'in_progress',
231	        'action=continue AND more phases in stage',
232	        ['advance current_phase via phase_advance'],
233	      ),
234	      outcome(
235	        'complete',
236	        'action=continue AND last phase in stage',
237	        ['stage completion', 'emit deps_satisfied cascade'],
238	      ),
239	      outcome(
240	        'in_progress',
241	        'action={goto:X} AND within max_revisits limit AND target phase in same stage',
242	        ['increment revisit counter', 'set current_phase', 'emit phase_start'],
243	      ),
244	      outcome(
245	        'awaiting_user',
246	        'action={goto:X} AND max_revisits limit exceeded',
247	        ['insert pending_attention{kind=revisit_limit}'],
248	      ),
249	      outcome(
250	        'awaiting_retry',
251	        'action={retry:...} AND retry_count < max',
252	        ['set next retry_mode'],
253	      ),
254	      outcome(
255	        'awaiting_user',
256	        'action=stop-and-ask',
257	        ['insert pending_attention'],
258	      ),
259	      outcome(
260	        'abandoned',
261	        'action=stop',
262	        ['cleanup worktree per config'],
263	      ),
264	      outcome(
265	        'awaiting_retry',
266	        'action={fail:...} AND retry budget > 0',
267	        ['record reason in events'],
268	      ),
269	      outcome(
270	        'awaiting_user',
271	        'action={fail:...} AND retry budget exhausted',
272	        ['record reason in events'],
273	      ),
274	    ]),
275	
276	    validator_fail: conditional([
277	      outcome(
278	        'awaiting_retry',
279	        'retry budget > 0',
280	        ['increment retry_count'],
281	      ),
282	    ]),
283	
284	    diff_check_fail: conditional([
285	      outcome(
286	        'awaiting_retry',
287	        'retry budget > 0, classifier=policy',
288	        ['log forbidden diff'],
289	      ),
290	    ]),
291	
292	    // session_fail outcomes are resolved by the failure classifier.
293	    // The Pipeline Engine calls classify(stderr, parseState) first, then
294	    // selects the matching outcome.  unknown always routes to awaiting_user.
295	    session_fail: conditional([
296	      outcome(
297	        'awaiting_retry',
298	        'classifier=transient AND retry budget > 0',
299	        ['record retry_history'],
300	      ),
301	      outcome(
302	        'awaiting_user',
303	        'classifier=permanent',
304	        ['insert pending_attention'],
305	      ),
306	      outcome(
307	        'awaiting_user',
308	        'classifier=unknown',
309	        [],
310	      ),
311	    ]),
312	
313	    retries_exhausted: direct('awaiting_user', []),
314	
315	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', ['SIGTERM process group']),
316	  },
317	
318	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
319	  // awaiting_retry
320	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
321	  awaiting_retry: {
322	    backoff_elapsed: conditional([
323	      outcome(
324	        'in_progress',
325	        'retry budget remaining',
326	        ['compute next retry_mode from ladder', 'assemble prompt'],
327	      ),
328	    ]),
329	    retries_exhausted: direct('awaiting_user', ['insert pending_attention']),
330	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', []),
331	  },
332	
333	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
334	  // rate_limited  (D61)
335	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
336	  rate_limited: {
337	    rate_limit_window_elapsed: direct(
338	      'in_progress',
339	      ['fresh session', 'seed handoff.json with last attempt'],
340	      'window reset_at ≤ now',
341	    ),
342	    user_retry: direct(
343	      'in_progress',
344	      ['fresh session'],
345	      'user forced early resume',
346	    ),
347	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', []),
348	    rate_limit_detected: direct(
349	      'rate_limited',
350	      ['extend timer', 'refresh reset_at'],
351	      'idempotent — refresh reset_at',
352	    ),
353	  },
354	
355	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
356	  // awaiting_user
357	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
358	  awaiting_user: {
359	    user_retry: direct(
360	      'in_progress',
361	      ['consume user_injected_context from handoff.json'],
362	    ),
363	    user_block: direct('blocked', ['record blocked_reason']),
364	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', []),
365	  },
366	
367	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
368	  // blocked
369	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
370	  blocked: {
371	    user_unblock_with_notes: direct(
372	      'in_progress',
373	      ['append handoff entry seeded with notes'],
374	    ),
375	    user_cancel: direct('abandoned', []),
376	    // no-op: blocking is manual, not dep-driven
377	    deps_satisfied: direct('blocked', []),
378	  },
379	
380	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
381	  // complete — terminal; only deps_satisfied is a no-op acknowledgement
382	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
383	  complete: {
384	    deps_satisfied: direct('complete', []),
385	  },
386	
387	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
388	  // abandoned — terminal; every event is a no-op (table: "abandoned | any")
389	  //
390	  // `Record<Event, TransitionResult>` here forces a compile error when a
391	  // new Event is added to the union without updating this row.
392	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
393	  abandoned: {
394	    deps_satisfied: ABANDONED_NOOP,
395	    phase_start: ABANDONED_NOOP,
396	    phase_advance: ABANDONED_NOOP,
397	    stage_complete: ABANDONED_NOOP,
398	    stage_approval_granted: ABANDONED_NOOP,
399	    pre_commands_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
400	    pre_command_failed: ABANDONED_NOOP,
401	    session_spawned: ABANDONED_NOOP,
402	    session_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
403	    session_fail: ABANDONED_NOOP,
404	    rate_limit_detected: ABANDONED_NOOP,
405	    rate_limit_window_elapsed: ABANDONED_NOOP,
406	    post_command_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
407	    post_command_action: ABANDONED_NOOP,
408	    validators_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
409	    validator_fail: ABANDONED_NOOP,
410	    diff_check_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
411	    diff_check_fail: ABANDONED_NOOP,
412	    retry_budget_remaining: ABANDONED_NOOP,
413	    retries_exhausted: ABANDONED_NOOP,
414	    backoff_elapsed: ABANDONED_NOOP,
415	    user_retry: ABANDONED_NOOP,
416	    user_block: ABANDONED_NOOP,
417	    user_unblock_with_notes: ABANDONED_NOOP,
418	    user_cancel: ABANDONED_NOOP,
419	    bootstrap_ok: ABANDONED_NOOP,
420	    bootstrap_fail: ABANDONED_NOOP,
421	  } satisfies Record<Event, TransitionResult>,
422	} as const;
423	
424	// Enforce RC-2: TRANSITIONS must not be mutated at runtime.
425	// Freeze each row, then the outer map.
426	for (const row of Object.values(TRANSITIONS)) {
427	  Object.freeze(row);
428	}
429	Object.freeze(TRANSITIONS);
430	
431	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
432	// transition() — pure lookup function
433	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	
435	/**
436	 * Look up the TransitionResult for a (state, event) pair.
437	 *
438	 * Returns `undefined` for any pair not listed in state-machine-transitions.md.
439	 * Never throws.
440	 *
441	 * Pure function: no I/O, no SQLite writes, no timer creation, no signals.
442	 * Identical inputs always return the identical TransitionResult reference.
443	 */
444	export function transition(state: State, event: Event): TransitionResult | undefined {
445	  return TRANSITIONS[state][event];
446	}
447

1	/**
2	 * State Machine — State and Event union types.
3	 *
4	 * Source of truth: docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md
5	 *
6	 * State: lifecycle position of an item within a pipeline stage.
7	 * Event: named trigger raised by Process Manager, Pre/Post Runner,
8	 *        Worktree Manager, retry ladder, or HTTP control.
9	 *
10	 * Compile-time exhaustiveness: TRANSITIONS in transitions.ts is typed as
11	 * `{ [S in State]: ... }`, so adding a new State without updating that
12	 * record produces a TypeScript error.
13	 */
14	
15	/** Lifecycle states an item can occupy within a stage. */
16	export type State =
17	  | 'pending'
18	  | 'ready'
19	  | 'bootstrapping'
20	  | 'bootstrap_failed'
21	  | 'in_progress'
22	  | 'awaiting_retry'
23	  | 'rate_limited'
24	  | 'awaiting_user'
25	  | 'blocked'
26	  | 'complete'
27	  | 'abandoned';
28	
29	/**
30	 * Events the Pipeline Engine recognises. Raised by concrete modules;
31	 * this is the closed set — the Engine ignores anything not listed here.
32	 */
33	export type Event =
34	  | 'deps_satisfied'
35	  | 'phase_start'
36	  | 'phase_advance'
37	  | 'stage_complete'
38	  | 'stage_approval_granted'
39	  | 'pre_commands_ok'
40	  | 'pre_command_failed'
41	  | 'session_spawned'
42	  | 'session_ok'
43	  | 'session_fail'
44	  | 'rate_limit_detected'
45	  | 'rate_limit_window_elapsed'
46	  | 'post_command_ok'
47	  | 'post_command_action'
48	  | 'validators_ok'
49	  | 'validator_fail'
50	  | 'diff_check_ok'
51	  | 'diff_check_fail'
52	  | 'retry_budget_remaining'
53	  | 'retries_exhausted'
54	  | 'backoff_elapsed'
55	  | 'user_retry'
56	  | 'user_block'
57	  | 'user_unblock_with_notes'
58	  | 'user_cancel'
59	  | 'bootstrap_ok'
60	  | 'bootstrap_fail';
61

summary: Read pipeline design docs and existing source files

content.text:
Here are all 5 files in their complete contents:

## 1. SQLite Schema (`sqlite-schema.sql`)

The schema defines 11 tables supporting a workflow orchestration system:
- **workflows**: Main workflow records with status, spec, pipeline config
- **items**: Task items within workflow stages (opaque user data separated from harness state)
- **sessions**: Agent session records with token usage tracking (input, output, cache creation/read tokens)
- **events**: Append-only trace log for state-machine transitions and debug
- **artifact_writes**: File provenance tracking per session
- **pending_attention**: Dashboard attention banner source (blocks, crashes, rate limits, user actions)
- **prepost_runs**: Pre/post command execution records with exit codes and stdout/stderr paths
- **schema_migrations**: Migration version tracking

Uses WAL mode, NORMAL sync, foreign keys ON, 5s busy_timeout. Single-writer pattern with reader-only connections.

---

## 2. State Machine Transitions (`transitions.ts`)

Defines the complete (State × Event) → TransitionResult lookup table. Key features:

**State types**: pending, ready, bootstrapping, bootstrap_failed, in_progress, awaiting_retry, rate_limited, awaiting_user, blocked, complete, abandoned

**Transition outcomes** include:
- Direct transitions (unconditional move to target state)
- Conditional transitions (multiple outcomes with guards evaluated in order)
- Side effects as declarative labels (e.g., "invoke Worktree Manager.create", "SIGTERM process group")

**Notable patterns**:
- `session_ok` is composite (accumulates validators_ok + diff_check_ok + post_command_ok before lookup)
- `post_command_action` handles continue/goto/retry/fail/stop-and-ask dispatch
- Terminal states (complete, abandoned) have guards/no-ops
- Immutable at runtime (Object.freeze on TRANSITIONS and all rows)

---

## 3. State Machine States (`states.ts`)

Simple union type definitions:

**State**: 11-member union of item lifecycle positions
**Event**: 27-member union of triggers (deps_satisfied, phase_start, phase_advance, session_ok, session_fail, rate_limit_detected, user_retry, user_block, bootstrap_ok/fail, etc.)

Both types are sourced from `docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md` and used to enforce exhaustiveness in TRANSITIONS.

---

## 4. Config Types (`config.ts`)

YAML configuration schema with two forms:

**RawConfig**: Direct YAML parse (relative paths as strings)
**ResolvedConfig**: All relative paths → absolute, plus `configDir` field

**Key sections**:
- **Action grammar**: RetryMode (continue, fresh_with_failure_summary, fresh_with_diff, awaiting_user); ActionValue (stop-and-ask, stop, {goto}, {retry}, {fail})
- **PrePostCommand**: name, run (argv), timeout_s, env, actions map (exit-code → ActionValue)
- **Phase**: command, args, env, cwd, prompt_template, output_artifacts, max_outer_retries, retry_ladder, heartbeat, pre/post commands
- **Stage**: id, run ('once' or 'per-item'), phases, needs_approval, items_from/list/id/depends_on, items_display
- **Top-level**: project, pipeline, phases map, worktrees (base_dir, bootstrap, teardown), notifications, github, retention, logging, runtime, rate_limit, ui, safety_mode

---

## 5. Database Pool (`db.ts`)

Exports `DbPool` interface and `openDbPool()` function:

**DbPool members**:
- `writer`: Single read-write connection with PRAGMAs (busy_timeout=5s, WAL, NORMAL sync, foreign_keys ON)
- `reader()`: Returns a pre-opened read-only connection (readonly: true enforced at connection level)
- `close()`: Closes both connections
- `transaction<T>(fn)`: Wraps fn in BEGIN/COMMIT, re-throws on error (better-sqlite3 handles rollback)

**PRAGMA strategy**:
- Writer gets all four (busy_timeout, journal_mode, synchronous, foreign_keys)
- Reader omits journal_mode and synchronous (already inherited from file; set on readonly conn risks SQLITE_READONLY)
- busy_timeout set BEFORE migrations bootstrap

No module-level singleton; callers inject the pool.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Failure classifier — categorises a session failure as one of:
3	 *   transient  — rate-limit, network glitch; retry with backoff
4	 *   permanent  — authentication/credential failure; requires user action
5	 *   policy     — content-policy or diff-check violation; requires user review
6	 *   unknown    — pattern not recognised; always routes to awaiting_user (D07)
7	 *
8	 * Source of truth: docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md §Guard mechanics
9	 *
10	 * This function has NO side effects.  It performs no SQLite writes,
11	 * no I/O, no timer creation.  Same inputs always produce the same result.
12	 *
13	 * Inputs:
14	 *   stderr     — captured stderr text from the failed session
15	 *   parseState — stream-json parse counters from the session (RC-3)
16	 *
17	 * The Pipeline Engine calls classify() before performing a transition
18	 * lookup on a session_fail event.
19	 */
20	
21	export type FailureClass = 'transient' | 'permanent' | 'policy' | 'unknown';
22	
23	/**
24	 * Stream-json parse state captured during the session.
25	 * Provides additional context beyond stderr alone (RC-3).
26	 */
27	export interface ParseState {
28	  /**
29	   * Number of lines that failed JSON.parse() during the session.
30	   * A non-zero value indicates a partially tainted stream.
31	   */
32	  parseErrors: number;
33	  /**
34	   * `type` field from the last successfully parsed stream-json event,
35	   * or null if the session produced no parseable output.
36	   */
37	  lastEventType: string | null;
38	}
39	
40	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	// Pattern tables
42	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	
44	/** Patterns that indicate a transient (retryable) failure. */
45	const TRANSIENT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
46	  /rate_limit_error/i,
47	  /overloaded_error/i,
48	  /rate\s+limit/i,
49	  /too many requests/i,
50	  /overloaded/i,
51	  /\b529\b/,          // Anthropic overload HTTP status
52	  /\b503\b/,          // service unavailable
53	  /\b502\b/,          // bad gateway
54	  /\b504\b/,          // gateway timeout
55	  /ECONNRESET/,
56	  /ETIMEDOUT/,
57	  /connection\s+reset/i,
58	  /network\s+timeout/i,
59	  /request\s+timeout/i,
60	];
61	
62	/** Patterns that indicate a permanent (non-retryable) failure. */
63	const PERMANENT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
64	  /authentication_error/i,
65	  /invalid_api_key/i,
66	  /permission_error/i,
67	  /authentication\s+failed/i,
68	  /\bunauthorized\b/i,
69	  /not\s+authorized/i,
70	  /invalid\s+api\s+key/i,
71	  /\bunauthenticated\b/i,
72	  /\b401\b/,           // HTTP 401 Unauthorized
73	  /\b403\b/,           // HTTP 403 Forbidden (permission)
74	];
75	
76	/** Patterns that indicate a policy (content/diff) failure. */
77	const POLICY_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
78	  /content_policy_violation/i,
79	  /content\s+policy/i,
80	  /policy\s+violation/i,
81	  /content\s+filter/i,
82	  /safety\s+filter/i,
83	];
84	
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	// classify()
87	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	
89	/**
90	 * Classify a session failure.
91	 *
92	 * Inspection order: transient → permanent → policy → unknown.
93	 * If the stderr string matches a pattern in one class, that class is
94	 * returned immediately without checking further classes.
95	 *
96	 * Parse-state influence (RC-3):
97	 *   - If no stderr pattern matches AND the session produced zero parseable
98	 *     events (lastEventType === null) AND the stream had parse errors, the
99	 *     failure likely represents a corrupted or wholly unparseable stream;
100	 *     this is classified as 'policy' to prevent automatic retry on a
101	 *     session whose output was never interpretable.
102	 *   - In all other unmatched cases 'unknown' is returned.
103	 *
104	 * 'unknown' always routes to awaiting_user (D07, state-machine-transitions.md
105	 * §Guard mechanics); there is no code path where 'unknown' enables a retry.
106	 */
107	export function classify(stderr: string, parseState: ParseState): FailureClass {
108	  if (matches(stderr, TRANSIENT_PATTERNS)) {
109	    return 'transient';
110	  }
111	
112	  if (matches(stderr, PERMANENT_PATTERNS)) {
113	    return 'permanent';
114	  }
115	
116	  if (matches(stderr, POLICY_PATTERNS)) {
117	    return 'policy';
118	  }
119	
120	  // Parse-state tiebreaker: a session that produced no parseable events at
121	  // all AND had stream parse errors is structurally corrupted.  Classify as
122	  // policy to avoid retrying an uninterpretable stream.
123	  if (parseState.parseErrors > 0 && parseState.lastEventType === null) {
124	    return 'policy';
125	  }
126	
127	  return 'unknown';
128	}
129	
130	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	// Internal helpers
132	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	
134	function matches(text: string, patterns: readonly RegExp[]): boolean {
135	  return patterns.some(p => p.test(text));
136	}
137

1	# State Machine Transition Table
2	
3	Source: plan-draft3.md §State Machine, §Retry, §Crash Recovery, §Phase
4	Pre/Post Commands (D50, D53, D61, D56).
5	Revised per beta3-review-feedback.md Issues 1–4: transitions scoped
6	within stages, review-specific events replaced with generic phase-outcome
7	events driven by post-command results, stage-level completion added.
8	
9	The Pipeline Engine is the only component that applies transitions. Every
10	transition is: `(from_state, event) → (to_state, side_effects, guard)`
11	committed inside a single SQLite transaction (`db.transaction(fn)()`) before
12	any observable side effect.
13	
14	## Scope
15	
16	Transitions operate at two levels (Issue 1):
17	
18	- **Item-level**: within a `per-item` stage, each item has its own state
19	  machine instance tracking progress through the stage's phase list.
20	- **Stage-level**: the workflow advances through stages sequentially. A
21	  stage completes when all its work is done (for `once` stages, when
22	  the single phase run completes; for `per-item` stages, when all items
23	  reach terminal states within that stage).
24	
25	## States (plan-draft3 §State Machine)
26	
27	| State | Meaning |
28	|---|---|
29	| `pending` | item not yet started (dependencies unmet, or stage not reached) |
30	| `ready` | dependencies complete, eligible to start |
31	| `bootstrapping` | worktree bootstrap running |
32	| `bootstrap_failed` | terminal until user action |
33	| `in_progress` | agent session active (any phase — implement, review, or user-defined) |
34	| `awaiting_retry` | between failed attempt and next attempt (backoff) |
35	| `rate_limited` | rate limit signaled; waiting with backoff (D61) |
36	| `awaiting_user` | retries exhausted or policy requires human decision |
37	| `blocked` | manual/dependency block |
38	| `complete` | all phases in the current stage completed successfully |
39	| `abandoned` | user cancelled or workflow aborted |
40	
41	Note: `review` and `review_invalid` are no longer distinct states (Issue 4).
42	Review is a phase like any other; an item in a review phase has state
43	`in_progress` with `current_phase` set to the review phase name. Validator
44	failures in any phase route through `awaiting_retry`, not a phase-specific
45	state.
46	
47	## Events
48	
49	Events are raised by concrete modules. This table is the closed set the
50	Pipeline Engine recognizes.
51	
52	| Event | Raised by | Payload summary |
53	|---|---|---|
54	| `deps_satisfied` | Pipeline Engine on cascade | item_id, satisfied dependency set |
55	| `phase_start` | Pipeline Engine (user command, auto-advance) | phase, retry_mode |
56	| `phase_advance` | Pipeline Engine after post commands | next phase in stage (Issue 1) |
57	| `stage_complete` | Pipeline Engine | stage_id, all items terminal (Issue 1) |
58	| `stage_approval_granted` | HTTP control | stage_id (Issue 1, Q-architecture-proposed-lifecycle) |
59	| `pre_commands_ok` | Pre/Post Runner | command list |
60	| `pre_command_failed` | Pre/Post Runner | name, exit_code, action |
61	| `session_spawned` | Process Manager | pid, pgid, session_id |
62	| `session_ok` | Process Manager | exit 0 + no error frames |
63	| `session_fail` | Process Manager | exit code, classifier tag |
64	| `rate_limit_detected` | Stream-JSON parser | reset_at (if present) |
65	| `rate_limit_window_elapsed` | rate_limit state timer | — |
66	| `post_command_ok` | Pre/Post Runner | command name |
67	| `post_command_action` | Pre/Post Runner | resolved action object |
68	| `validators_ok` | Artifact validators | paths |
69	| `validator_fail` | Artifact validators | path, ajv errors |
70	| `diff_check_ok` | Pipeline Engine | items_from file unchanged |
71	| `diff_check_fail` | Pipeline Engine | disallowed-diff summary |
72	| `retry_budget_remaining` | retry-ladder | next_mode, next_attempt |
73	| `retries_exhausted` | retry-ladder | final classifier |
74	| `backoff_elapsed` | awaiting_retry timer | — |
75	| `user_retry` | HTTP control | commandId, optional inject |
76	| `user_block` | HTTP control | commandId, reason |
77	| `user_unblock_with_notes` | HTTP control | commandId, notes |
78	| `user_cancel` | HTTP control | commandId |
79	| `bootstrap_ok` | Worktree Manager | — |
80	| `bootstrap_fail` | Worktree Manager | error detail |
81	
82	Events removed from the prior revision (Issue 4): `review_verdict_parsed`,
83	`review_verdict_missing`. Review pass/fail is now determined by `post:`
84	commands, which raise `post_command_ok` / `post_command_action` events.
85	
86	Guards and side effects are written as text below the table. They are not
87	prose "maybes" — every branch a concrete module can cause is enumerated.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Transition table
92	
93	Format: `(from, event) → to | guard | side_effects`. "side_effects" are
94	always additive; SQLite writes are implicit in every transition. A `—` in
95	guard means the transition is unconditional for that `(from, event)` pair.
96	
97	### pending / ready
98	
99	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
100	|---|---|---|---|---|
101	| `pending` | `deps_satisfied` | `ready` | all `depends_on` items have status ∈ {complete} | enqueue item for scheduling |
102	| `pending` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | cascade-cancel dependents |
103	| `ready` | `phase_start` | `bootstrapping` | worktree_path null or absent | invoke Worktree Manager.create |
104	| `ready` | `phase_start` | `in_progress` | worktree already exists | skip bootstrap, spawn agent |
105	| `ready` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
106	
107	### bootstrapping / bootstrap_failed
108	
109	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
110	|---|---|---|---|---|
111	| `bootstrapping` | `bootstrap_ok` | `in_progress` (same txn, then phase_start) | — | write worktree_path, branch_name |
112	| `bootstrapping` | `bootstrap_fail` | `bootstrap_failed` | — | insert `pending_attention{kind=bootstrap_failed}`, no auto-cleanup (§Worktree Management) |
113	| `bootstrap_failed` | `user_retry` | `bootstrapping` | user action recorded | re-run bootstrap |
114	| `bootstrap_failed` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | run teardown, remove worktree |
115	
116	### in_progress
117	
118	All phase types (implement, review, user-defined) share this state.
119	`current_phase` distinguishes which phase is running (Issue 4).
120	
121	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
122	|---|---|---|---|---|
123	| `in_progress` | `pre_command_failed` → action `fail` | `awaiting_retry` or `awaiting_user` | per retry_ladder | log to events + prepost_runs |
124	| `in_progress` | `pre_command_failed` → action `stop-and-ask` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
125	| `in_progress` | `pre_commands_ok` | `in_progress` | — | spawn agent session |
126	| `in_progress` | `session_spawned` | `in_progress` | — | stream-json parser attached |
127	| `in_progress` | `rate_limit_detected` | `rate_limited` | — | suppress heartbeat, schedule resume |
128	| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + `validators_ok` + `diff_check_ok` + all `post_command_ok` + more phases in stage | `in_progress` | all post actions were `continue` | advance `current_phase` to next phase in stage, spawn next session (`phase_advance` event) (Issue 1) |
129	| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + `validators_ok` + `diff_check_ok` + all `post_command_ok` + last phase in stage | `complete` | all post actions were `continue` | check for stage completion, unlock dependents via `deps_satisfied` cascade |
130	| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + any `post_command_action != continue` | per action (see below) | action map | execute goto/retry/stop/fail |
131	| `in_progress` | `validator_fail` | `awaiting_retry` | retry budget > 0 | increment retry_count |
132	| `in_progress` | `diff_check_fail` | `awaiting_retry` | retry budget > 0, classifier = `policy` | log forbidden diff (§File Contract D10) |
133	| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `transient` + budget | `awaiting_retry` | exponential-backoff window | record retry_history |
134	| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `permanent` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
135	| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `unknown` | `awaiting_user` | — | default safe |
136	| `in_progress` | `retries_exhausted` | `awaiting_user` | — | — |
137	| `in_progress` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | SIGTERM process group |
138	
139	### post_command_action dispatch
140	
141	These rows expand the `session_ok + post_command_action` branch above.
142	Post commands drive all phase-outcome routing, including what was formerly
143	review-specific logic (Issue 4). A review phase's post command that checks
144	verdicts and exits non-zero triggers `goto: implement` (or equivalent),
145	replacing the former harness-level `review_verdict_parsed → fail` path.
146	
147	| From | Action | To | Guard | Side effects |
148	|---|---|---|---|---|
149	| `in_progress` | `continue` | fallthrough to next phase or `complete` (if last) | — | — |
150	| `in_progress` | `{goto: <phase>}` | `in_progress` for `<phase>` | `max_revisits > current_visits`, target phase is in same stage (Issue 1) | increment revisit counter, set `current_phase`, phase_start |
151	| `in_progress` | `{retry: {mode, max}}` | `awaiting_retry` | current retry_count < max | set next retry_mode |
152	| `in_progress` | `stop-and-ask` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
153	| `in_progress` | `stop` | `abandoned` | — | cleanup worktree per config |
154	| `in_progress` | `{fail: {reason}}` | `awaiting_retry` if budget else `awaiting_user` | — | record reason in events |
155	| any | goto exceeds `max_revisits` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention{kind=revisit_limit} |
156	
157	### awaiting_retry
158	
159	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
160	|---|---|---|---|---|
161	| `awaiting_retry` | `backoff_elapsed` | `in_progress` | retry budget remaining | compute next retry_mode from ladder, assemble prompt |
162	| `awaiting_retry` | `retries_exhausted` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
163	| `awaiting_retry` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
164	
165	### rate_limited (D61)
166	
167	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
168	|---|---|---|---|---|
169	| `rate_limited` | `rate_limit_window_elapsed` | `in_progress` | window reset_at ≤ now | fresh session (not `-c`); handoff.json seeded with last attempt |
170	| `rate_limited` | `user_retry` | `in_progress` | user forced early resume | fresh session |
171	| `rate_limited` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
172	| `rate_limited` | `rate_limit_detected` | `rate_limited` | idempotent — refresh reset_at | extend timer |
173	
174	### awaiting_user / blocked / complete / abandoned
175	
176	| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
177	|---|---|---|---|---|
178	| `awaiting_user` | `user_retry` | `in_progress` (fresh) | — | consume `user_injected_context` from handoff.json |
179	| `awaiting_user` | `user_block` | `blocked` | — | record blocked_reason |
180	| `awaiting_user` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
181	| `blocked` | `user_unblock_with_notes` | `in_progress` (fresh) | — | append handoff entry seeded with notes |
182	| `blocked` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
183	| `blocked` | `deps_satisfied` | `blocked` | — | no-op (blocking is manual, not dep-driven) |
184	| `complete` | `deps_satisfied` | `complete` | — | no-op |
185	| `abandoned` | any | `abandoned` | — | no-op |
186	
187	### Cascade on terminal transitions (D08, D59)
188	
189	A transition to `awaiting_user`, `blocked`, or `abandoned` triggers a
190	cascading `blocked` with `blocked_reason: "dependency <item-id> <status>"`
191	on every transitive dependent, in the same SQLite transaction. Items
192	with no dependency relation continue to execute in topological order.
193	
194	### Stage-level transitions (Issue 1)
195	
196	| Trigger | Guard | Side effect |
197	|---|---|---|
198	| All items in a `per-item` stage reach terminal states (`complete`, `blocked`, or `abandoned`) | — | Emit `stage_complete` event; advance `workflows.current_stage` to next stage |
199	| `once` stage phase completes (session_ok + post commands pass) | — | Emit `stage_complete`; advance to next stage |
200	| Next stage has `needs_approval: true` | — | Enter `awaiting_user` at workflow level; insert `pending_attention{kind=stage_needs_approval}` |
201	| `stage_approval_granted` for workflow in `awaiting_user` | stage matches | Start the next stage |
202	| No more stages | — | Workflow status → terminal (`completed`, `completed_with_blocked`, or `abandoned` depending on item statuses) |
203	
204	Stage-level goto is deferred beyond v1 (Issue 1 — agreed direction).
205	
206	---
207	
208	## Guard mechanics
209	
210	- **Retry budget**: phase-level `max_outer_retries` minus `items.retry_count`
211	  in the current retry window; the window starts at the first transient
212	  failure and resets on successful phase completion.
213	- **Backoff**: exponential with jitter, upper bound at `retry_window_start +
214	  N` seconds where N comes from the classifier policy (transient only).
215	  Wall-clock timer handled by a single node-side scheduler — not a SQLite
216	  trigger.
217	- **Classifier**: the failure classifier consumes stderr + stream-json
218	  parse state + event context and returns one of
219	  `{transient, permanent, policy, unknown}`. `unknown` always routes to
220	  `awaiting_user` (D07).
221	- **max_revisits** (D53): tracked per `(item_id, destination phase)`
222	  in a `pending_attention.payload` counter or equivalent in-memory
223	  projection seeded from `events` on restart. `goto` targets are
224	  constrained to phases within the same stage (Issue 1).
225	
226	---
227	
228	## Test assertion list (unit + integration)
229	
230	These assertions live in `src/server/state-machine/transitions.test.ts`
231	(unit) and `tests/integration/state-machine.spec.ts`. Each corresponds to
232	a failure mode table row (plan-draft3 §Failure Modes) or a v1 Acceptance
233	scenario (§v1 Acceptance).
234	
235	1. Every `(state, event)` pair in this document has a defined row.
236	   Completeness test iterates the cartesian product and fails on any
237	   missing pair.
238	2. No `from → to` row targets a state not in the state enum.
239	3. `pending → ready` triggers only when all depends_on are `complete`;
240	   mixed states leave item in `pending`.
241	4. `ready → bootstrapping → in_progress` runs inside one logical
242	   scheduling tick (no intermediate external side effects visible).
243	5. `bootstrap_failed` never auto-transitions; only `user_retry` or
244	   `user_cancel` can leave it.
245	6. `in_progress → complete` (last phase in stage) requires `session_ok ∧
246	   validators_ok ∧ diff_check_ok ∧ all post commands resolved to
247	   continue` (Issue 4 — no review-specific requirement).
248	7. `in_progress → awaiting_retry` on transient failure increments
249	   `items.retry_count` exactly once.
250	8. `in_progress → rate_limited` suppresses the heartbeat "stalled"
251	   warning for the duration of the state (D61, §Heartbeat).
252	9. `rate_limited → in_progress` only on `rate_limit_window_elapsed` or
253	   explicit `user_retry`; never on `session_ok` (no session to observe).
254	10. Phase advancement (`phase_advance`) within a stage does not reset
255	    the item's status — it stays `in_progress` with `current_phase`
256	    updated atomically (Issue 1).
257	11. `post_command_action={goto: X}` transitions to `in_progress` for
258	    X; `max_revisits` counter increments and trips at the configured
259	    bound. Target must be in the same stage (Issue 1).
260	12. `post_command_action=stop-and-ask` always lands in `awaiting_user`
261	    with a `pending_attention` row; banner visible until acknowledged.
262	13. `user_cancel` is accepted from every non-terminal state and always
263	    terminates in `abandoned`.
264	14. Terminal transitions of an item cascade-block every transitive
265	    dependent in the same transaction.
266	15. Every transition writes an `events` row with the same
267	    `(workflow_id, item_id, session_id, stage, phase)` correlation set
268	    used by structured logging.
269	16. On harness restart, transitions recorded in SQLite are idempotent:
270	    replaying the recovery path produces zero state delta.
271	17. `pre_command_failed` with no matching action key falls through to
272	    `"*"`; absent `"*"` is a config-validation error at load time.
273	18. Stage completion fires only when ALL items in the stage are terminal
274	    (complete, blocked, or abandoned). A single non-terminal item
275	    prevents stage advancement (Issue 1).
276	19. Stage with `needs_approval: true` inserts `pending_attention` and
277	    pauses until `stage_approval_granted` (Q-architecture-proposed-lifecycle).
278	
279	---
280	
281	## Restart projection
282	
283	Plan-draft3 §Crash Recovery: on restart, the Pipeline Engine rebuilds its
284	short-lived projection by:
285	
286	1. Load all workflows with non-terminal status.
287	2. For each item, read its latest `session` row and probe
288	   `kill(pid, 0)`; any stale PID marks its session `cancelled` and the
289	   item lands in `awaiting_user` (default, D60) or `in_progress`
290	   (`auto_resume: true`).
291	3. For items in any phase at crash, delete partial output files
292	   (e.g., `reviews/item-*/*` for a review phase) before re-running.
293	4. For items in `rate_limited`, reseat the window timer from the
294	   last known `reset_at` on the corresponding `events` row.
295	5. Write a `recovery_state` JSON on each affected workflow for the
296	   dashboard banner (D27).
297	
298	No transition is applied during restart itself — restart only sets
299	`recovery_state`; the user's subsequent `user_retry` or `auto_resume`
300	fires the normal `(awaiting_user, user_retry) → in_progress` row.

Perfect! I have all the information. Here are the complete file contents and directory listings:

---

## File Contents

### 1. /Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md

```
# State Machine Transition Table

Source: plan-draft3.md §State Machine, §Retry, §Crash Recovery, §Phase
Pre/Post Commands (D50, D53, D61, D56).
Revised per beta3-review-feedback.md Issues 1–4: transitions scoped
within stages, review-specific events replaced with generic phase-outcome
events driven by post-command results, stage-level completion added.

The Pipeline Engine is the only component that applies transitions. Every
transition is: `(from_state, event) → (to_state, side_effects, guard)`
committed inside a single SQLite transaction (`db.transaction(fn)()`) before
any observable side effect.

## Scope

Transitions operate at two levels (Issue 1):

- **Item-level**: within a `per-item` stage, each item has its own state
  machine instance tracking progress through the stage's phase list.
- **Stage-level**: the workflow advances through stages sequentially. A
  stage completes when all its work is done (for `once` stages, when
  the single phase run completes; for `per-item` stages, when all items
  reach terminal states within that stage).

## States (plan-draft3 §State Machine)

| State | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `pending` | item not yet started (dependencies unmet, or stage not reached) |
| `ready` | dependencies complete, eligible to start |
| `bootstrapping` | worktree bootstrap running |
| `bootstrap_failed` | terminal until user action |
| `in_progress` | agent session active (any phase — implement, review, or user-defined) |
| `awaiting_retry` | between failed attempt and next attempt (backoff) |
| `rate_limited` | rate limit signaled; waiting with backoff (D61) |
| `awaiting_user` | retries exhausted or policy requires human decision |
| `blocked` | manual/dependency block |
| `complete` | all phases in the current stage completed successfully |
| `abandoned` | user cancelled or workflow aborted |

Note: `review` and `review_invalid` are no longer distinct states (Issue 4).
Review is a phase like any other; an item in a review phase has state
`in_progress` with `current_phase` set to the review phase name. Validator
failures in any phase route through `awaiting_retry`, not a phase-specific
state.

## Events

Events are raised by concrete modules. This table is the closed set the
Pipeline Engine recognizes.

| Event | Raised by | Payload summary |
|---|---|---|
| `deps_satisfied` | Pipeline Engine on cascade | item_id, satisfied dependency set |
| `phase_start` | Pipeline Engine (user command, auto-advance) | phase, retry_mode |
| `phase_advance` | Pipeline Engine after post commands | next phase in stage (Issue 1) |
| `stage_complete` | Pipeline Engine | stage_id, all items terminal (Issue 1) |
| `stage_approval_granted` | HTTP control | stage_id (Issue 1, Q-architecture-proposed-lifecycle) |
| `pre_commands_ok` | Pre/Post Runner | command list |
| `pre_command_failed` | Pre/Post Runner | name, exit_code, action |
| `session_spawned` | Process Manager | pid, pgid, session_id |
| `session_ok` | Process Manager | exit 0 + no error frames |
| `session_fail` | Process Manager | exit code, classifier tag |
| `rate_limit_detected` | Stream-JSON parser | reset_at (if present) |
| `rate_limit_window_elapsed` | rate_limit state timer | — |
| `post_command_ok` | Pre/Post Runner | command name |
| `post_command_action` | Pre/Post Runner | resolved action object |
| `validators_ok` | Artifact validators | paths |
| `validator_fail` | Artifact validators | path, ajv errors |
| `diff_check_ok` | Pipeline Engine | items_from file unchanged |
| `diff_check_fail` | Pipeline Engine | disallowed-diff summary |
| `retry_budget_remaining` | retry-ladder | next_mode, next_attempt |
| `retries_exhausted` | retry-ladder | final classifier |
| `backoff_elapsed` | awaiting_retry timer | — |
| `user_retry` | HTTP control | commandId, optional inject |
| `user_block` | HTTP control | commandId, reason |
| `user_unblock_with_notes` | HTTP control | commandId, notes |
| `user_cancel` | HTTP control | commandId |
| `bootstrap_ok` | Worktree Manager | — |
| `bootstrap_fail` | Worktree Manager | error detail |

Events removed from the prior revision (Issue 4): `review_verdict_parsed`,
`review_verdict_missing`. Review pass/fail is now determined by `post:`
commands, which raise `post_command_ok` / `post_command_action` events.

Guards and side effects are written as text below the table. They are not
prose "maybes" — every branch a concrete module can cause is enumerated.

---

## Transition table

Format: `(from, event) → to | guard | side_effects`. "side_effects" are
always additive; SQLite writes are implicit in every transition. A `—` in
guard means the transition is unconditional for that `(from, event)` pair.

### pending / ready

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `pending` | `deps_satisfied` | `ready` | all `depends_on` items have status ∈ {complete} | enqueue item for scheduling |
| `pending` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | cascade-cancel dependents |
| `ready` | `phase_start` | `bootstrapping` | worktree_path null or absent | invoke Worktree Manager.create |
| `ready` | `phase_start` | `in_progress` | worktree already exists | skip bootstrap, spawn agent |
| `ready` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |

### bootstrapping / bootstrap_failed

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bootstrapping` | `bootstrap_ok` | `in_progress` (same txn, then phase_start) | — | write worktree_path, branch_name |
| `bootstrapping` | `bootstrap_fail` | `bootstrap_failed` | — | insert `pending_attention{kind=bootstrap_failed}`, no auto-cleanup (§Worktree Management) |
| `bootstrap_failed` | `user_retry` | `bootstrapping` | user action recorded | re-run bootstrap |
| `bootstrap_failed` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | run teardown, remove worktree |

### in_progress

All phase types (implement, review, user-defined) share this state.
`current_phase` distinguishes which phase is running (Issue 4).

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `in_progress` | `pre_command_failed` → action `fail` | `awaiting_retry` or `awaiting_user` | per retry_ladder | log to events + prepost_runs |
| `in_progress` | `pre_command_failed` → action `stop-and-ask` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
| `in_progress` | `pre_commands_ok` | `in_progress` | — | spawn agent session |
| `in_progress` | `session_spawned` | `in_progress` | — | stream-json parser attached |
| `in_progress` | `rate_limit_detected` | `rate_limited` | — | suppress heartbeat, schedule resume |
| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + `validators_ok` + `diff_check_ok` + all `post_command_ok` + more phases in stage | `in_progress` | all post actions were `continue` | advance `current_phase` to next phase in stage, spawn next session (`phase_advance` event) (Issue 1) |
| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + `validators_ok` + `diff_check_ok` + all `post_command_ok` + last phase in stage | `complete` | all post actions were `continue` | check for stage completion, unlock dependents via `deps_satisfied` cascade |
| `in_progress` | `session_ok` + any `post_command_action != continue` | per action (see below) | action map | execute goto/retry/stop/fail |
| `in_progress` | `validator_fail` | `awaiting_retry` | retry budget > 0 | increment retry_count |
| `in_progress` | `diff_check_fail` | `awaiting_retry` | retry budget > 0, classifier = `policy` | log forbidden diff (§File Contract D10) |
| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `transient` + budget | `awaiting_retry` | exponential-backoff window | record retry_history |
| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `permanent` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
| `in_progress` | `session_fail` + classifier `unknown` | `awaiting_user` | — | default safe |
| `in_progress` | `retries_exhausted` | `awaiting_user` | — | — |
| `in_progress` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | SIGTERM process group |

### post_command_action dispatch

These rows expand the `session_ok + post_command_action` branch above.
Post commands drive all phase-outcome routing, including what was formerly
review-specific logic (Issue 4). A review phase's post command that checks
verdicts and exits non-zero triggers `goto: implement` (or equivalent),
replacing the former harness-level `review_verdict_parsed → fail` path.

| From | Action | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `in_progress` | `continue` | fallthrough to next phase or `complete` (if last) | — | — |
| `in_progress` | `{goto: <phase>}` | `in_progress` for `<phase>` | `max_revisits > current_visits`, target phase is in same stage (Issue 1) | increment revisit counter, set `current_phase`, phase_start |
| `in_progress` | `{retry: {mode, max}}` | `awaiting_retry` | current retry_count < max | set next retry_mode |
| `in_progress` | `stop-and-ask` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
| `in_progress` | `stop` | `abandoned` | — | cleanup worktree per config |
| `in_progress` | `{fail: {reason}}` | `awaiting_retry` if budget else `awaiting_user` | — | record reason in events |
| any | goto exceeds `max_revisits` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention{kind=revisit_limit} |

### awaiting_retry

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `awaiting_retry` | `backoff_elapsed` | `in_progress` | retry budget remaining | compute next retry_mode from ladder, assemble prompt |
| `awaiting_retry` | `retries_exhausted` | `awaiting_user` | — | insert pending_attention |
| `awaiting_retry` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |

### rate_limited (D61)

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `rate_limited` | `rate_limit_window_elapsed` | `in_progress` | window reset_at ≤ now | fresh session (not `-c`); handoff.json seeded with last attempt |
| `rate_limited` | `user_retry` | `in_progress` | user forced early resume | fresh session |
| `rate_limited` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
| `rate_limited` | `rate_limit_detected` | `rate_limited` | idempotent — refresh reset_at | extend timer |

### awaiting_user / blocked / complete / abandoned

| From | Event | To | Guard | Side effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `awaiting_user` | `user_retry` | `in_progress` (fresh) | — | consume `user_injected_context` from handoff.json |
| `awaiting_user` | `user_block` | `blocked` | — | record blocked_reason |
| `awaiting_user` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
| `blocked` | `user_unblock_with_notes` | `in_progress` (fresh) | — | append handoff entry seeded with notes |
| `blocked` | `user_cancel` | `abandoned` | — | — |
| `blocked` | `deps_satisfied` | `blocked` | — | no-op (blocking is manual, not dep-driven) |
| `complete` | `deps_satisfied` | `complete` | — | no-op |
| `abandoned` | any | `abandoned` | — | no-op |

### Cascade on terminal transitions (D08, D59)

A transition to `awaiting_user`, `blocked`, or `abandoned` triggers a
cascading `blocked` with `blocked_reason: "dependency <item-id> <status>"`
on every transitive dependent, in the same SQLite transaction. Items
with no dependency relation continue to execute in topological order.

### Stage-level transitions (Issue 1)

| Trigger | Guard | Side effect |
|---|---|---|
| All items in a `per-item` stage reach terminal states (`complete`, `blocked`, or `abandoned`) | — | Emit `stage_complete` event; advance `workflows.current_stage` to next stage |
| `once` stage phase completes (session_ok + post commands pass) | — | Emit `stage_complete`; advance to next stage |
| Next stage has `needs_approval: true` | — | Enter `awaiting_user` at workflow level; insert `pending_attention{kind=stage_needs_approval}` |
| `stage_approval_granted` for workflow in `awaiting_user` | stage matches | Start the next stage |
| No more stages | — | Workflow status → terminal (`completed`, `completed_with_blocked`, or `abandoned` depending on item statuses) |

Stage-level goto is deferred beyond v1 (Issue 1 — agreed direction).

---

## Guard mechanics

- **Retry budget**: phase-level `max_outer_retries` minus `items.retry_count`
  in the current retry window; the window starts at the first transient
  failure and resets on successful phase completion.
- **Backoff**: exponential with jitter, upper bound at `retry_window_start +
  N` seconds where N comes from the classifier policy (transient only).
  Wall-clock timer handled by a single node-side scheduler — not a SQLite
  trigger.
- **Classifier**: the failure classifier consumes stderr + stream-json
  parse state + event context and returns one of
  `{transient, permanent, policy, unknown}`. `unknown` always routes to
  `awaiting_user` (D07).
- **max_revisits** (D53): tracked per `(item_id, destination phase)`
  in a `pending_attention.payload` counter or equivalent in-memory
  projection seeded from `events` on restart. `goto` targets are
  constrained to phases within the same stage (Issue 1).

---

## Test assertion list (unit + integration)

These assertions live in `src/server/state-machine/transitions.test.ts`
(unit) and `tests/integration/state-machine.spec.ts`. Each corresponds to
a failure mode table row (plan-draft3 §Failure Modes) or a v1 Acceptance
scenario (§v1 Acceptance).

1. Every `(state, event)` pair in this document has a defined row.
   Completeness test iterates the cartesian product and fails on any
   missing pair.
2. No `from → to` row targets a state not in the state enum.
3. `pending → ready` triggers only when all depends_on are `complete`;
   mixed states leave item in `pending`.
4. `ready → bootstrapping → in_progress` runs inside one logical
   scheduling tick (no intermediate external side effects visible).
5. `bootstrap_failed` never auto-transitions; only `user_retry` or
   `user_cancel` can leave it.
6. `in_progress → complete` (last phase in stage) requires `session_ok ∧
   validators_ok ∧ diff_check_ok ∧ all post commands resolved to
   continue` (Issue 4 — no review-specific requirement).
7. `in_progress → awaiting_retry` on transient failure increments
   `items.retry_count` exactly once.
8. `in_progress → rate_limited` suppresses the heartbeat "stalled"
   warning for the duration of the state (D61, §Heartbeat).
9. `rate_limited → in_progress` only on `rate_limit_window_elapsed` or
   explicit `user_retry`; never on `session_ok` (no session to observe).
10. Phase advancement (`phase_advance`) within a stage does not reset
    the item's status — it stays `in_progress` with `current_phase`
    updated atomically (Issue 1).
11. `post_command_action={goto: X}` transitions to `in_progress` for
    X; `max_revisits` counter increments and trips at the configured
    bound. Target must be in the same stage (Issue 1).
12. `post_command_action=stop-and-ask` always lands in `awaiting_user`
    with a `pending_attention` row; banner visible until acknowledged.
13. `user_cancel` is accepted from every non-terminal state and always
    terminates in `abandoned`.
14. Terminal transitions of an item cascade-block every transitive
    dependent in the same transaction.
15. Every transition writes an `events` row with the same
    `(workflow_id, item_id, session_id, stage, phase)` correlation set
    used by structured logging.
16. On harness restart, transitions recorded in SQLite are idempotent:
    replaying the recovery path produces zero state delta.
17. `pre_command_failed` with no matching action key falls through to
    `"*"`; absent `"*"` is a config-validation error at load time.
18. Stage completion fires only when ALL items in the stage are terminal
    (complete, blocked, or abandoned). A single non-terminal item
    prevents stage advancement (Issue 1).
19. Stage with `needs_approval: true` inserts `pending_attention` and
    pauses until `stage_approval_granted` (Q-architecture-proposed-lifecycle).

---

## Restart projection

Plan-draft3 §Crash Recovery: on restart, the Pipeline Engine rebuilds its
short-lived projection by:

1. Load all workflows with non-terminal status.
2. For each item, read its latest `session` row and probe
   `kill(pid, 0)`; any stale PID marks its session `cancelled` and the
   item lands in `awaiting_user` (default, D60) or `in_progress`
   (`auto_resume: true`).
3. For items in any phase at crash, delete partial output files
   (e.g., `reviews/item-*/*` for a review phase) before re-running.
4. For items in `rate_limited`, reseat the window timer from the
   last known `reset_at` on the corresponding `events` row.
5. Write a `recovery_state` JSON on each affected workflow for the
   dashboard banner (D27).

No transition is applied during restart itself — restart only sets
`recovery_state`; the user's subsequent `user_retry` or `auto_resume`
fires the normal `(awaiting_user, user_retry) → in_progress` row.
```

### 2. /Users/jforsythe/dev/ai/yoke/src/server/state-machine/classifier.ts

```typescript
/**
 * Failure classifier — categorises a session failure as one of:
 *   transient  — rate-limit, network glitch; retry with backoff
 *   permanent  — authentication/credential failure; requires user action
 *   policy     — content-policy or diff-check violation; requires user review
 *   unknown    — pattern not recognised; always routes to awaiting_user (D07)
 *
 * Source of truth: docs/design/state-machine-transitions.md §Guard mechanics
 *
 * This function has NO side effects.  It performs no SQLite writes,
 * no I/O, no timer creation.  Same inputs always produce the same result.
 *
 * Inputs:
 *   stderr     — captured stderr text from the failed session
 *   parseState — stream-json parse counters from the session (RC-3)
 *
 * The Pipeline Engine calls classify() before performing a transition
 * lookup on a session_fail event.
 */

export type FailureClass = 'transient' | 'permanent' | 'policy' | 'unknown';

/**
 * Stream-json parse state captured during the session.
 * Provides additional context beyond stderr alone (RC-3).
 */
export interface ParseState {
  /**
   * Number of lines that failed JSON.parse() during the session.
   * A non-zero value indicates a partially tainted stream.
   */
  parseErrors: number;
  /**
   * `type` field from the last successfully parsed stream-json event,
   * or null if the session produced no parseable output.
   */
  lastEventType: string | null;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Pattern tables
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/** Patterns that indicate a transient (retryable) failure. */
const TRANSIENT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
  /rate_limit_error/i,
  /overloaded_error/i,
  /rate\s+limit/i,
  /too many requests/i,
  /overloaded/i,
  /\b529\b/,          // Anthropic overload HTTP status
  /\b503\b/,          // service unavailable
  /\b502\b/,          // bad gateway
  /\b504\b/,          // gateway timeout
  /ECONNRESET/,
  /ETIMEDOUT/,
  /connection\s+reset/i,
  /network\s+timeout/i,
  /request\s+timeout/i,
];

/** Patterns that indicate a permanent (non-retryable) failure. */
const PERMANENT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
  /authentication_error/i,
  /invalid_api_key/i,
  /permission_error/i,
  /authentication\s+failed/i,
  /\bunauthorized\b/i,
  /not\s+authorized/i,
  /invalid\s+api\s+key/i,
  /\bunauthenticated\b/i,
  /\b401\b/,           // HTTP 401 Unauthorized
  /\b403\b/,           // HTTP 403 Forbidden (permission)
];

/** Patterns that indicate a policy (content/diff) failure. */
const POLICY_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
  /content_policy_violation/i,
  /content\s+policy/i,
  /policy\s+violation/i,
  /content\s+filter/i,
  /safety\s+filter/i,
];

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// classify()
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

/**
 * Classify a session failure.
 *
 * Inspection order: transient → permanent → policy → unknown.
 * If the stderr string matches a pattern in one class, that class is
 * returned immediately without checking further classes.
 *
 * Parse-state influence (RC-3):
 *   - If no stderr pattern matches AND the session produced zero parseable
 *     events (lastEventType === null) AND the stream had parse errors, the
 *     failure likely represents a corrupted or wholly unparseable stream;
 *     this is classified as 'policy' to prevent automatic retry on a
 *     session whose output was never interpretable.
 *   - In all other unmatched cases 'unknown' is returned.
 *
 * 'unknown' always routes to awaiting_user (D07, state-machine-transitions.md
 * §Guard mechanics); there is no code path where 'unknown' enables a retry.
 */
export function classify(stderr: string, parseState: ParseState): FailureClass {
  if (matches(stderr, TRANSIENT_PATTERNS)) {
    return 'transient';
  }

  if (matches(stderr, PERMANENT_PATTERNS)) {
    return 'permanent';
  }

  if (matches(stderr, POLICY_PATTERNS)) {
    return 'policy';
  }

  // Parse-state tiebreaker: a session that produced no parseable events at
  // all AND had stream parse errors is structurally corrupted.  Classify as
  // policy to avoid retrying an uninterpretable stream.
  if (parseState.parseErrors > 0 && parseState.lastEventType === null) {
    return 'policy';
  }

  return 'unknown';
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Internal helpers
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

function matches(text: string, patterns: readonly RegExp[]): boolean {
  return patterns.some(p => p.test(text));
}
```
